rows.title: Actors
rows.old_rationale: While live theater and physical presence on set remain human-centric, the occupation is highly exposed due to AI's ability to generate digital likenesses, synthesize voices, and replace background extras or voiceover artists. The core work product—audio and video—is increasingly susceptible to generative AI, leading to significant industry restructuring and potential displacement in digital media productions.
rows.new_rationale: Acting is fundamentally rooted in physical presence, live performance, and complex human expression, which are difficult to replicate entirely through digital means. While digital tools are used in production and voiceover work, the core of the profession relies on human creativity, interpersonal collaboration, and the physical embodiment of characters.

rows.title: Models
rows.old_rationale: While runway and live event modeling require physical presence, the majority of modeling work results in digital or print imagery which is highly vulnerable to AI-generated synthetic media. AI can now create hyper-realistic virtual models or 'digital twins' of existing models, allowing brands to showcase clothing and products without physical photo shoots, significantly reducing the demand for human labor in commercial and catalog sectors.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is the physical presence and appearance of the individual, which remains highly dependent on the real world despite the increasing use of digital media for distribution. While AI-generated imagery poses a significant threat to the digital aspects of the role, the job itself relies on physical attributes and live interaction rather than routine information processing or complex decision-making.

rows.title: Kindergarten and elementary school teachers
rows.old_rationale: AI will significantly automate back-end knowledge work such as lesson planning, grading, and administrative communication, which currently occupy a large portion of a teacher's time. However, the core of the role involves physical supervision, emotional support, and real-time behavioral management of young children, which creates a substantial barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While teachers use digital tools for planning and communication, the core of the role is fundamentally rooted in physical, in-person interaction and the management of young children. The job requires high-stakes, real-time judgment and deep interpersonal engagement that is difficult to automate or delegate to digital systems.

rows.title: High school teachers
rows.old_rationale: High school teachers are heavily exposed because core digital tasks like lesson planning, content creation, and grading are highly susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. While the physical requirement of classroom management and interpersonal mentorship provides a buffer, AI's ability to provide personalized tutoring and automated feedback significantly reshapes the instructional delivery and administrative workload of the profession.
rows.new_rationale: While teachers increasingly use digital tools for lesson planning and communication, the core of the role is inherently social, requiring high-stakes interpersonal engagement, empathy, and classroom management that cannot be automated. The physical presence required to supervise, instruct, and adapt to the immediate emotional and behavioral needs of students makes this occupation highly resistant to AI displacement.

rows.title: Middle school teachers
rows.old_rationale: Middle school teachers have high exposure because a significant portion of their workload—lesson planning, grading, and content creation—is digital and highly susceptible to AI automation. However, the core of the job requires physical presence for classroom management, emotional support for adolescents, and real-time interpersonal interaction, which provides a substantial buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While teachers increasingly use digital tools for administration and lesson delivery, the core of the role is deeply rooted in physical classroom presence and high-stakes, real-time human interaction. The job requires complex, non-routine judgment regarding student development, social-emotional support, and classroom management that is inherently difficult to automate.

rows.title: Substance abuse, behavioral disorder, and mental health counselors
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a mix of high-level knowledge work and intense interpersonal interaction. While AI can significantly assist with digital tasks like documentation, treatment planning, and monitoring progress, the core of the job requires human empathy, crisis intervention, and the navigation of complex emotional and physical environments that AI cannot yet replicate.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this profession relies on deep human empathy, trust, and real-time interpersonal interaction, which are highly resistant to automation. While documentation is digital, the primary work product is the therapeutic relationship and high-stakes clinical judgment regarding client safety and mental health.

rows.title: Nurse anesthetists, nurse midwives, and nurse practitioners
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of high-level knowledge work, such as diagnosing conditions and analyzing test results, which is highly susceptible to AI assistance. However, the core of the job requires physical presence for exams, procedures (like delivering babies or administering anesthesia), and complex interpersonal communication that provides a strong buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While APRNs use digital systems for records and diagnostic data, the core of their work is highly physical, involving direct patient care, physical exams, and the operation of medical equipment. The role is defined by high-stakes clinical judgment and deep interpersonal engagement, both of which are essential to patient safety and outcomes, making the work highly resistant to automation.

rows.title: School and career counselors and advisors
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a mix of high-touch interpersonal work and digital information processing. AI can significantly automate the 'hard' data tasks like analyzing student performance trends, matching aptitudes to career paths, and drafting résumés, but the core functions of emotional support, crisis intervention (reporting abuse), and navigating complex human relationships in a school setting provide a strong buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While counselors use digital tools for record-keeping and data analysis, the core of the role is deeply rooted in high-stakes, empathetic, and face-to-face human interaction. The necessity for professional judgment in sensitive situations, such as reporting abuse or navigating complex personal crises, makes this role highly resistant to full automation.

rows.title: Elementary, middle, and high school principals
rows.old_rationale: Principals perform significant knowledge work—such as curriculum development, budget management, and data analysis of student performance—that is highly susceptible to AI augmentation and automation. However, the core of the role requires physical presence for school safety, real-time crisis management, and high-stakes interpersonal interactions with students, parents, and staff that AI cannot currently replicate.
rows.new_rationale: While principals use digital tools for reporting and scheduling, the core of the role is inherently physical and site-specific, requiring constant presence in a school environment. The job is defined by high-stakes human interaction, leadership, and accountability for the safety and development of students, which are deeply complex tasks that are difficult to delegate to AI.

rows.title: Psychologists
rows.old_rationale: Psychology is a knowledge-based profession where core tasks like analyzing data, writing reports, and researching are highly susceptible to AI enhancement. However, the critical requirements for human empathy, ethical judgment, and real-time interpersonal observation in clinical and school settings create a significant barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this profession relies on deep interpersonal connection, empathy, and high-stakes clinical judgment that is inherently human-centric. While documentation and research involve digital tools, the primary work product—therapeutic intervention and behavioral assessment—requires physical presence and nuanced human interaction that is difficult to automate.

rows.title: Speech-language pathologists
rows.old_rationale: Speech-language pathology involves a significant amount of digital-adjacent work, such as analyzing speech patterns, documenting progress, and developing treatment plans, all of which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement. However, the core of the job requires physical presence for swallowing therapy, real-time interpersonal coaching, and high-stakes clinical judgment that limits full automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role involves direct, physical, and highly empathetic human interaction, including manual manipulation for swallowing therapy and real-time behavioral coaching. While documentation is digital, the therapeutic process relies on professional clinical judgment and interpersonal trust that is difficult to automate or delegate.

rows.title: Producers and directors
rows.old_rationale: Producers and directors are highly exposed because their core work products—scripts, visual aesthetics, and edited video—are increasingly being generated or enhanced by AI tools. While the role requires significant human leadership and physical presence on set, the rapid advancement of generative AI in pre-production (scripting/storyboarding) and post-production (editing/VFX) allows a single director to do the work of a much larger team, potentially consolidating the industry.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the post-production and administrative work is digital, the core of the role involves high-stakes creative leadership, on-set physical coordination, and complex human management. The job relies heavily on subjective artistic vision, negotiation, and accountability for the final product, which are difficult to automate.

rows.title: Art directors
rows.old_rationale: Art directors work in a fundamentally digital domain where AI is rapidly advancing in image generation, layout design, and video synthesis. While the role requires high-level human judgment, leadership, and client communication, the core work product can be heavily augmented or automated by AI, significantly increasing productivity and potentially reducing the number of directors needed to oversee creative projects.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the design output is digital, the role is heavily centered on high-level creative vision, leadership, and complex client negotiations that require human empathy and subjective judgment. The work involves managing teams and interpreting abstract client needs, which are difficult to automate, though the technical execution of the designs they oversee is increasingly digital.

rows.title: Craft and fine artists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical craftsmanship and digital creation. While craft artists (potters, glassblowers) and sculptors have low exposure due to the physical nature of their work, digital artists and illustrators face very high exposure as AI can now generate high-quality imagery and complex designs from text prompts. The overall score reflects this split, as AI significantly impacts the commercial and digital segments of the field while leaving traditional physical mediums largely intact.
rows.new_rationale: While some artists use digital tools, the core of this occupation relies on physical manipulation of materials, manual dexterity, and site-specific studio work. The work is highly creative and subjective rather than routine, and success depends heavily on personal reputation, client relationships, and the unique, non-delegable judgment of the individual artist.

rows.title: Music directors and composers
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of high-exposure digital work (composing) and lower-exposure physical work (conducting). Generative AI is rapidly automating music composition, arrangement, and scoring for commercial media, while the music director role remains more resilient due to the need for live leadership, physical rehearsals, and human-to-human artistic interpretation.
rows.new_rationale: While composition increasingly utilizes digital software, the core of the role relies heavily on physical, real-time coordination of live musicians and the high-stakes, subjective interpretation of artistic performance. The work is deeply rooted in human interaction, leadership, and nuanced creative judgment that is difficult to automate, despite the increasing use of digital tools in the creative process.

rows.title: Adult basic and secondary education and ESL teachers
rows.old_rationale: The core work product—language instruction, literacy training, and test preparation—is fundamentally digital and information-based, making it highly susceptible to AI-driven tutoring and translation tools. While the role requires significant cultural sensitivity and interpersonal motivation, AI can now provide personalized lesson plans, real-time feedback, and adaptive learning paths that significantly increase teacher productivity or replace routine instructional tasks.
rows.new_rationale: While these teachers use digital tools for lesson planning and documentation, the core of the role relies heavily on in-person instruction, empathy, and the ability to adapt to diverse student needs in real-time. The high dependency on human interaction and the professional judgment required to assess and support adult learners make this role less susceptible to full automation.

rows.title: Computer and information research scientists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is fundamentally digital, involving high-level coding, algorithm design, and data analysis—all areas where AI is rapidly advancing. While these scientists are the ones building AI, the tools they create are increasingly capable of automating their own core tasks, such as writing code, simplifying algorithms, and analyzing experimental results, leading to extreme productivity gains and role restructuring.
rows.new_rationale: While the work is almost entirely digital, it focuses on high-level innovation, theoretical development, and complex problem-solving rather than routine information processing. The role requires significant high-stakes judgment and accountability for creating foundational technologies, though it involves moderate levels of human collaboration and occasional physical interaction with hardware or robotics.

rows.title: Set and exhibit designers
rows.old_rationale: The core creative process—including script analysis, conceptual sketching, and CAD modeling—is increasingly susceptible to generative AI and automated design tools that can rapidly produce 3D environments and visual concepts. While the role requires physical-world considerations like material budgets and on-site installation oversight, the shift toward digital production (such as virtual sets and LED volumes) and AI-assisted drafting significantly increases productivity and reduces the total human labor required per project.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the design and drafting process is digitized via CAD software, the core output is a physical, site-specific installation that requires manual coordination and real-world implementation. The role relies heavily on human collaboration with directors and curators to translate abstract narratives into tangible spaces, requiring high-level creative judgment that is difficult to fully automate.

rows.title: Industrial designers
rows.old_rationale: Industrial design is a predominantly digital occupation where core tasks like sketching, 3D modeling, and rendering are being rapidly transformed by generative AI and advanced CAD automation. While the role requires physical prototyping and cross-functional collaboration, the vast majority of the design process is computer-based, making it highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and workflow restructuring.
rows.new_rationale: Industrial design relies heavily on digital CAD/CAID software for modeling and production instructions, yet it remains deeply rooted in physical prototyping and real-world manufacturing constraints. The role requires significant human interaction for client consultation and cross-functional collaboration, alongside high-stakes professional judgment regarding product safety, usability, and economic feasibility.

rows.title: Umpires, referees, and other sports officials
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical presence and objective data processing. While computer vision and AI can already automate 'calls' (like strike zones or out-of-bounds) with higher accuracy than humans, the role still requires physical presence to manage player conduct, ensure safety, and handle real-time interpersonal disputes on the field.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is physical presence and real-time, high-stakes human judgment within a dynamic, non-digital environment. While video technology is increasingly used for support, the primary responsibilities—managing participants, enforcing rules, and navigating interpersonal conflict—are deeply tied to the physical world and human interaction.

rows.title: Arbitrators, mediators, and conciliators
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is primarily digital and information-based, involving the analysis of legal documents, evidence, and the drafting of settlement agreements—tasks where AI is rapidly achieving high proficiency. While the role requires significant interpersonal skills and emotional intelligence to manage confrontational parties, the core analytical and administrative functions are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and partial automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role relies on high-stakes human interaction, empathy, and neutral judgment, which are difficult to automate. While they process information and documents, the primary value is in facilitating complex, emotionally charged interpersonal negotiations that require professional accountability and nuanced decision-making.

rows.title: Top executives
rows.old_rationale: Top executives perform high-level knowledge work including financial analysis, strategic planning, and contract review, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the core of the role involves human-centric responsibilities like leadership, high-stakes negotiation, and accountability to boards or the public, which provide a significant buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While top executives utilize digital tools for analysis and communication, their core value lies in high-stakes decision-making, strategic leadership, and complex interpersonal negotiation. The role is defined by accountability for organizational outcomes and the management of human relationships, which are inherently difficult to delegate to AI.

rows.title: Customer service representatives
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this role—answering questions, processing orders, and resolving complaints—are fundamentally digital and information-based, making them highly susceptible to AI-driven automation. Large Language Models and AI agents are already capable of handling complex natural language interactions, and the BLS specifically projects a decline in employment due to automation in this sector.
rows.new_rationale: The role is highly digital and involves significant routine information processing, such as accessing databases and following standardized procedures, making it a primary target for automation. While human interaction is a core component, much of the routine inquiry resolution is increasingly handled by AI, leaving only complex or emotionally sensitive tasks for human representatives.

rows.title: Janitors and building cleaners
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are almost entirely physical, involving manual labor in unpredictable and varied environments like schools, hospitals, and construction sites. While AI might assist with minor administrative tasks like supply ordering or scheduling, the primary work of cleaning, disinfecting, and making physical repairs remains a significant barrier to AI and robotic automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is manual labor and physical interaction with the built environment, which is highly resistant to AI automation. While there is minimal digital or information-processing work, the job requires significant physical presence and dexterity that current robotics struggle to replicate in unstructured, real-world settings.

rows.title: Heavy and tractor-trailer truck drivers
rows.old_rationale: While the core task of driving is physical and occurs in a complex real-world environment, it is a primary target for autonomous vehicle AI, which could eventually automate long-haul highway segments. However, the job still requires significant physical presence for non-driving tasks like securing cargo, conducting inspections, and navigating 'last-mile' urban environments that remain challenging for current AI.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is the physical operation of a heavy vehicle in the real world, which is highly resistant to automation despite the use of digital logs and dispatch systems. While the work involves significant accountability for safety, cargo, and legal compliance, it requires minimal interpersonal interaction and relies primarily on physical skills and sensory perception.

rows.title: Wholesale and manufacturing sales representatives
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is heavily focused on information processing, communication, and data analysis, all of which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement and automation. While high-stakes B2B negotiations and physical trade shows provide a buffer, AI can now handle lead generation, personalized outreach, price optimization, and technical product explanations, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the total number of representatives needed.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the administrative and analytical work is digital, the core of the role relies heavily on high-stakes interpersonal persuasion, relationship building, and negotiation. The physical dependency is significant due to the necessity of site visits, product demonstrations, and in-person client management, which limits the extent to which AI can fully automate the role.

rows.title: Teacher assistants
rows.old_rationale: Teacher assistants have moderate exposure because AI can significantly automate their administrative and instructional support tasks, such as grading, lesson preparation, and providing personalized tutoring through AI agents. However, a large portion of the role involves physical supervision, behavioral management, and assisting students with special needs or personal hygiene, which require a physical human presence that AI cannot replace.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is highly dependent on physical presence, direct supervision, and interpersonal interaction with students, which cannot be replaced by digital tools. While some tasks like recordkeeping are routine, the primary value lies in the human-centric, real-time support provided to students in a classroom or care environment.

rows.title: Postsecondary teachers
rows.old_rationale: Postsecondary teaching is primarily knowledge work involving research, writing, and curriculum design—all areas where AI is highly capable. While the role requires significant human interaction and mentorship, AI can automate grading, lesson planning, and literature reviews, potentially increasing individual productivity to the point of restructuring faculty needs.
rows.new_rationale: Postsecondary teaching involves significant digital interaction for research and administration, but the core of the role relies heavily on high-stakes human interaction, mentorship, and expert judgment that is difficult to automate. While some information processing is routine, the pedagogical and research components require complex, non-routine cognitive engagement and sustained interpersonal relationships.

rows.title: Grounds maintenance workers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are almost entirely physical and performed in unpredictable outdoor environments, which provides a strong natural barrier against AI. While autonomous mowers or drones may assist with specific tasks, the majority of the work—such as planting, tree climbing, and complex manual maintenance—requires human dexterity and physical presence that AI cannot replicate.
rows.new_rationale: The work is almost entirely physical, involving manual labor, operation of heavy machinery, and direct interaction with outdoor environments. It requires minimal digital engagement or information processing, as the core tasks are focused on the physical maintenance of landscapes and vegetation.

rows.title: Market research analysts
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this role—data collection, statistical analysis, trend forecasting, and report generation—are fundamentally digital and align perfectly with AI's strengths in processing large datasets and generating insights. While human judgment is still needed for high-level strategy and client relationships, AI can now automate the vast majority of the technical and administrative tasks that define the daily work of an analyst.
rows.new_rationale: The core work of market research analysts is heavily centered on digital data collection, statistical analysis, and the creation of digital reports and visualizations. While the role requires human interaction for presenting findings and coordinating with stakeholders, the analytical and processing components are highly susceptible to automation through AI-driven data tools.

rows.title: Lawyers
rows.old_rationale: Lawyers perform high-level knowledge work that is almost entirely digital, involving document drafting, legal research, and complex data analysis—all areas where LLMs excel. While courtroom advocacy and high-stakes interpersonal negotiation provide a protective barrier, the vast majority of billable hours are spent on tasks that AI can now significantly accelerate or automate, leading to major structural changes in the profession.
rows.new_rationale: While lawyers perform significant routine information processing and research that is highly digital, the core of the profession relies on high-stakes human interaction, persuasion, and ethical accountability. The requirement for professional judgment and the necessity of navigating complex, non-routine human conflicts make the role highly resistant to full automation despite the digital nature of legal documentation.

rows.title: Licensed practical and licensed vocational nurses
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical tasks such as bathing, dressing, and wound care, which are currently beyond the reach of AI and robotics. However, AI will significantly impact the information-processing aspects of the job, including documenting patient care, monitoring vitals, and assisting with diagnostic reporting, leading to moderate overall exposure.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is highly dependent on physical presence, manual patient care, and direct human interaction, which are difficult to automate. While documentation and information processing are part of the job, they are secondary to the high-stakes, real-world clinical responsibilities that require professional judgment and physical intervention.

rows.title: Special education teachers
rows.old_rationale: AI will significantly streamline the digital and administrative aspects of the role, such as drafting Individualized Education Programs (IEPs), adapting lesson plans, and tracking student data. However, the core of the job requires intense physical presence, emotional intelligence, and real-time behavioral management for students with complex disabilities, which AI cannot replicate.
rows.new_rationale: While special education teachers use digital tools for documentation and lesson planning, the core of the role is highly physical and deeply dependent on complex, real-time human interaction and emotional support. The work requires high-stakes professional judgment in adapting individualized education programs (IEPs) to meet the unique, evolving needs of students, which is difficult to automate or delegate.

rows.title: Bus drivers
rows.old_rationale: While autonomous driving technology is a direct long-term threat to the core task of vehicle operation, the role involves significant physical and interpersonal responsibilities that AI cannot yet replicate. School bus drivers must manage student behavior and safety, while transit and charter drivers assist passengers with disabilities and handle physical luggage, creating a substantial barrier to full automation in the near term.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is the physical operation of a vehicle in real-world environments, which is highly resistant to digital automation. While there is a significant human element in passenger management and safety, the work involves minimal digital information processing, focusing instead on high-stakes physical accountability and safety compliance.

rows.title: Real estate brokers and sales agents
rows.old_rationale: Real estate agents perform a mix of digital knowledge work and physical/interpersonal tasks. AI can automate significant portions of their digital workload, such as market analysis, property descriptions, lead generation, and contract preparation, which increases individual productivity and may reduce the total number of agents needed. However, the core of the job still requires physical property showings, high-stakes human negotiation, and local networking, which provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the administrative and listing work is digital, the core of the profession relies heavily on physical property showings and high-stakes, trust-based interpersonal negotiations. The role requires significant professional judgment in advising clients and navigating complex legal and financial transactions that are difficult to fully automate.

rows.title: Securities, commodities, and financial services sales agents
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital and information-based, involving data analysis, market monitoring, and financial modeling—all areas where AI is rapidly achieving parity with human experts. While high-level relationship management and complex negotiation (like M&A) provide some insulation, the core tasks of identifying investment opportunities, executing trades, and providing routine financial advice are highly susceptible to automation and significant productivity gains that could reduce the need for human agents.
rows.new_rationale: The core work is highly digital, involving constant interaction with financial data and electronic trading systems. While routine information processing is significant, the role is heavily dependent on high-stakes human relationships, trust, and professional judgment, which are difficult to fully automate.

rows.title: Postal service workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, such as delivering parcels and navigating outdoor environments, which provides a natural barrier to AI. However, AI and advanced automation are significantly impacting the sorting and processing components of the job, while the shift toward digital communication reduces overall mail volume and labor demand.
rows.new_rationale: While the occupation involves routine information processing and sorting, the core of the work is heavily dependent on the physical manipulation and delivery of tangible goods in the real world. Human interaction is limited to basic customer service, and the role requires minimal high-stakes judgment, making it highly susceptible to automation in sorting but resistant to full replacement due to the physical nature of last-mile delivery.

rows.title: Pharmacy technicians
rows.old_rationale: The role involves a significant amount of physical labor, such as measuring medications, packaging prescriptions, and interacting with patients in person, which provides a buffer against full automation. However, AI and automated dispensing systems are highly capable of handling the information-processing tasks like insurance claims, inventory management, and prescription data entry, which constitute a large portion of the daily workload.
rows.new_rationale: Pharmacy technicians perform highly routine, digital-heavy tasks like data entry and insurance processing, which are increasingly automated. However, the role remains heavily dependent on physical interaction with medications and patients, and carries significant accountability for accuracy in a clinical setting that requires human oversight.

rows.title: Property, real estate, and community association managers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical site management. While AI can automate significant portions of the job—such as drafting leases, financial reporting, budgeting, and coordinating maintenance requests—the role still requires physical inspections of facilities and high-stakes interpersonal conflict resolution. The exposure is high because the administrative and analytical 'office' tasks that comprise a large part of the day are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains.
rows.new_rationale: While the role involves significant digital record-keeping and financial reporting, the core of the job is deeply rooted in physical site inspections and high-stakes interpersonal interactions. Success depends heavily on empathy, conflict resolution, and on-site presence to manage facilities and human relationships, which are difficult to fully automate.

rows.title: Taxi drivers, shuttle drivers, and chauffeurs
rows.old_rationale: While the core task of driving is highly susceptible to automation through autonomous vehicle technology (AI), the job currently requires significant physical presence for tasks like loading luggage, assisting passengers with disabilities, and performing vehicle maintenance. The transition to fully driverless fleets faces regulatory and technical hurdles in complex environments, but the long-term trajectory for this digital-adjacent physical task is one of high potential displacement.
rows.new_rationale: While digital platforms are increasingly used for dispatching and navigation, the core of the job is physical transportation, which requires real-world navigation, vehicle maintenance, and manual assistance to passengers. The role involves significant human interaction and high-stakes responsibility for passenger safety, but the primary task of driving remains a physical-world activity.

rows.title: Advertising, promotions, and marketing managers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is predominantly digital and information-based, involving market research, data analysis, and content strategy—all areas where AI is rapidly advancing. While high-level strategic decision-making and stakeholder management provide some insulation, AI can now automate significant portions of campaign planning, creative drafting, and consumer data processing, leading to major restructuring of the role.
rows.new_rationale: The role is highly digital and involves significant information processing, such as analyzing market data and managing digital campaigns, which are susceptible to automation. However, the core of the job relies heavily on high-stakes human interaction, strategic persuasion, and professional judgment in managing client relationships and creative vision, which are difficult to fully delegate to AI.

rows.title: Financial analysts
rows.old_rationale: Financial analysts perform work that is almost entirely digital, involving the processing of large datasets, trend analysis, and the generation of reports—all areas where AI excels. While high-level strategy and client relationships provide some insulation, the core tasks of data evaluation, risk assessment, and financial modeling are being rapidly automated or significantly enhanced by AI, leading to major restructuring of the occupation.
rows.new_rationale: Financial analysts operate almost entirely within digital environments, processing vast amounts of structured financial data and economic trends. While the analytical and routine processing components are highly susceptible to automation, the role retains significant human-centric requirements for client communication, stakeholder management, and high-stakes professional judgment regarding investment risks and strategies.

rows.title: Painters, construction and maintenance
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are entirely physical, requiring manual dexterity, mobility at heights, and real-time adaptation to unpredictable physical environments. While AI might assist with peripheral business tasks like estimating or scheduling, it cannot perform the labor-intensive tasks of surface preparation and paint application.
rows.new_rationale: The work of a painter is overwhelmingly physical, requiring manual labor, site-specific navigation, and the operation of tools in real-world environments. While there is some minor information processing involved in calculating materials and communicating with clients, the core tasks are manual and highly resistant to digital or AI-driven automation.

rows.title: Public relations specialists
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of this occupation—writing press releases, drafting speeches, creating social media content, and monitoring public sentiment—are digital-first and align perfectly with the capabilities of Large Language Models. While high-level strategy and real-time interpersonal crisis management provide some protection, the vast majority of the daily work product can be generated or significantly optimized by AI, leading to high exposure and potential workforce consolidation.
rows.new_rationale: Public relations work is highly digital and involves significant information processing, such as drafting content and monitoring social media. However, the core of the role relies heavily on high-stakes human interaction, persuasion, and professional judgment in managing sensitive organizational reputations, which are difficult to fully automate.

rows.title: Coaches and scouts
rows.old_rationale: While AI is significantly impacting the analytical side of the job—such as processing player data, optimizing game strategy, and analyzing opponent film—the core of the occupation remains deeply physical and interpersonal. Coaches must provide real-time motivation, physical instruction, and human leadership on the field, which cannot be replicated by digital tools.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is deeply rooted in physical, real-world interaction, athlete development, and high-stakes interpersonal motivation, which are difficult to automate. While digital tools are used for record-keeping and video analysis, the primary value is derived from human leadership, physical presence at competitions, and the subjective judgment required to evaluate talent and manage team dynamics.

rows.title: Mechanical engineers
rows.old_rationale: Mechanical engineering is heavily reliant on digital tools like CAD, simulations, and data analysis, all of which are being rapidly enhanced by AI to automate complex design iterations and predictive modeling. While the role requires physical prototyping, site visits, and hardware testing, the core intellectual work of designing and analyzing mechanical systems is increasingly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and automation.
rows.new_rationale: Mechanical engineers rely heavily on digital tools like CAD and simulation software for design and analysis, but their work is fundamentally anchored in the physical world of manufacturing, testing, and hardware integration. While they perform structured analytical tasks, the role requires high-stakes professional judgment and accountability for safety and performance, which limits the extent to which these processes can be fully automated.

rows.title: Electrical and electronics engineers
rows.old_rationale: The core work of designing circuits, modeling systems, and writing documentation is digital and highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and optimization. While physical testing, site visits, and manufacturing supervision provide a buffer, the rapid advancement of AI in CAD, simulation, and coding significantly increases the productivity and exposure of the knowledge-work components.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the design and modeling work is digital, the role is deeply anchored in the physical world through testing, manufacturing supervision, and site-specific troubleshooting. The high level of accountability for safety, reliability, and complex system integration requires significant professional judgment that is difficult to automate.

rows.title: Telecommunications technicians
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical labor, including climbing towers, installing hardware, and manual troubleshooting in unpredictable environments, which provides a strong barrier against AI automation. While AI can assist with remote diagnostics, network optimization, and predictive maintenance, the physical necessity of splicing cables and mounting equipment ensures the role remains primarily human-driven.
rows.new_rationale: While the work involves managing digital infrastructure and signals, the core of the job is highly physical, requiring manual labor, climbing, and on-site installation in diverse environments. The role involves some routine troubleshooting and documentation, but the primary value is derived from physical manipulation of hardware and site-specific problem-solving that cannot be automated.

rows.title: Graphic designers
rows.old_rationale: Graphic design is a fundamentally digital occupation where core tasks—image generation, layout creation, and photo editing—are being rapidly transformed by generative AI. While high-level creative direction and client management remain human-centric, the automation of production tasks significantly increases individual productivity, likely reducing the total number of designers needed for routine commercial work.
rows.new_rationale: Graphic design is highly digital, relying on specialized software for the vast majority of production tasks. While the work involves significant creative judgment and client-facing collaboration, much of the execution involves structured layout and design processes that are increasingly susceptible to automation.

rows.title: Industrial production managers
rows.old_rationale: This role is a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical oversight. AI will significantly enhance the analytical aspects of the job, such as production scheduling, budget optimization, and predictive maintenance data analysis, but the core requirements of physical plant presence, personnel management, and real-time problem-solving in a manual environment provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While these managers use digital tools for data analysis and reporting, their work is fundamentally tied to the physical environment of a manufacturing plant and the management of human teams. The role requires high-stakes judgment regarding safety, budget, and operational efficiency that is deeply embedded in the physical and social context of the facility.

rows.title: Instructional coordinators
rows.old_rationale: Instructional coordinators perform high-level knowledge work including curriculum development, data analysis, and educational material review, all of which are digital-first tasks highly susceptible to AI augmentation. While the role requires significant interpersonal interaction for teacher coaching and stakeholder management, the core 'product'—curricula and instructional strategies—can be generated and optimized much faster using generative AI and advanced analytics.
rows.new_rationale: Instructional coordinators rely heavily on digital data analysis and curriculum design, but their core value lies in the high-stakes, human-centric work of mentoring teachers and navigating school politics. While data processing is a significant part of the role, the necessity for interpersonal influence and professional judgment in educational settings makes the job difficult to fully automate.

rows.title: Social and community service managers
rows.old_rationale: This role involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, such as grant writing, data analysis, and budget management, which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the core responsibilities of community stakeholder engagement, staff supervision, and navigating complex human crises provide a buffer that requires physical presence and high-level emotional intelligence.
rows.new_rationale: While this role involves significant administrative and data-reporting tasks that are digital and routine, the core of the work is deeply rooted in human interaction, stakeholder management, and community-based service delivery. The high level of accountability for program outcomes and the necessity of navigating complex, sensitive human situations make this role highly dependent on professional judgment that is difficult to automate.

rows.title: Tutors
rows.old_rationale: Tutors primarily engage in knowledge transfer, explanation, and feedback—tasks where generative AI and personalized learning algorithms excel. While the interpersonal and motivational aspects of tutoring provide some protection, the core work product is digital/informational, and AI's ability to provide 24/7, low-cost, personalized instruction for standardized tests and core subjects creates significant displacement risk.
rows.new_rationale: While tutoring involves significant information processing that is increasingly susceptible to AI automation, the core of the role relies heavily on human-centric skills like empathy, motivation, and real-time pedagogical adjustment. The work requires high levels of interpersonal engagement and professional judgment to tailor instruction to individual student needs, which remains difficult to fully delegate to digital systems.

rows.title: Architectural and engineering managers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is predominantly knowledge-based and digital, involving project planning, budgeting, and technical oversight that can be significantly enhanced by AI tools. While the role requires high-level human judgment, leadership, and physical site visits, the core tasks of analyzing technical data, scheduling, and coordinating complex engineering workflows are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and automation.
rows.new_rationale: While these managers rely heavily on digital tools for planning and design, their core value lies in high-stakes leadership, team coordination, and complex decision-making that requires deep domain expertise. Their work is deeply tied to physical-world outcomes like construction and manufacturing, necessitating a blend of technical oversight and interpersonal management that is difficult to fully automate.

rows.title: Computer network architects
rows.old_rationale: The core work of designing, documenting, and configuring networks is fundamentally digital and data-driven, making it highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and optimization tools. While physical hardware deployment and complex stakeholder management provide a slight buffer, AI is rapidly advancing in automated network topology design, security configuration, and predictive troubleshooting, which will significantly increase individual productivity and restructure the role.
rows.new_rationale: The work is highly digital and involves significant information processing, but it requires high-level architectural judgment and accountability for system stability that is difficult to fully automate. While there is some physical interaction with hardware in server rooms, the core value lies in the strategic design, security, and complex problem-solving required for organizational infrastructure.

rows.title: Roofers
rows.old_rationale: Roofing is a highly physical occupation performed in unpredictable outdoor environments that require manual dexterity, balance, and heavy lifting. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like drone-based inspections or material estimation, the core work of installing and repairing shingles and membranes remains entirely dependent on human labor.
rows.new_rationale: Roofing is a highly manual, site-specific trade that requires significant physical labor, dexterity, and navigation of complex physical environments. The work involves minimal digital interaction or routine information processing, as the core tasks are focused on the physical installation and repair of building materials.

rows.title: Medical scientists
rows.old_rationale: Medical scientists perform high-level knowledge work including data analysis, grant writing, and literature reviews, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation and automation. While physical laboratory work and clinical trials provide a buffer, the core of the profession is shifting toward computational biology and AI-driven drug discovery, significantly increasing productivity and reshaping research methodologies.
rows.new_rationale: While medical scientists rely heavily on digital tools for data analysis and publication, their core work is deeply rooted in physical laboratory experimentation, handling biological samples, and clinical trials. The role requires high-stakes professional judgment and accountability for research outcomes and safety, which are difficult to fully automate despite the routine nature of some data processing tasks.

rows.title: Physician assistants
rows.old_rationale: Physician assistants perform a mix of high-level knowledge work and physical clinical tasks. While AI will significantly augment diagnostic accuracy, treatment planning, and administrative documentation, the role requires physical presence for examinations, surgical procedures, and hands-on treatments like stitching wounds, as well as complex interpersonal counseling that is difficult to automate.
rows.new_rationale: While PAs use digital systems for records and diagnostic interpretation, the core of the role is deeply rooted in physical patient interaction, manual procedures, and high-stakes clinical judgment. The necessity for empathy, direct physical examination, and professional accountability for patient health outcomes makes this role highly resistant to automation.

rows.title: Meeting, convention, and event planners
rows.old_rationale: A significant portion of the role involves digital tasks such as logistics planning, vendor research, contract review, and communications, which are highly susceptible to AI automation and productivity gains. However, the core of the job requires physical site inspections, real-time on-site problem solving during events, and high-stakes interpersonal negotiation and relationship management, which provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the planning and logistics are handled digitally, the core of the role requires physical presence at venues and high-stakes, real-time coordination of events. Success is heavily dependent on complex human relationships, negotiation, and the ability to solve unpredictable, site-specific problems that require professional judgment.

rows.title: Photographers
rows.old_rationale: Photography is a hybrid role where the physical act of capturing real-world events (weddings, news, portraits) provides a buffer, but the digital output is highly vulnerable to AI. Generative AI can now create high-quality commercial and stock imagery from scratch and automate complex post-production tasks, significantly reducing the billable hours and demand for professional photographers in commercial and creative sectors.
rows.new_rationale: Photography is highly dependent on physical presence and real-world interaction to capture subjects, yet the post-production workflow is heavily digitized and increasingly automated. While the creative and interpersonal aspects of working with clients provide some protection, the core technical output is increasingly subject to digital manipulation and AI-driven enhancement tools.

rows.title: Respiratory therapists
rows.old_rationale: Respiratory therapy involves a significant amount of physical, hands-on patient care, such as performing chest physiotherapy, intubating patients, and maintaining equipment in real-time clinical environments. While AI will heavily assist in diagnostic analysis, ventilator setting optimization, and patient monitoring, the core of the job requires physical presence and interpersonal interaction that cannot be digitized.
rows.new_rationale: Respiratory therapy is a highly physical, hands-on profession requiring direct patient interaction, manual manipulation of equipment, and physical presence in clinical settings. While they use digital tools for monitoring and documentation, the core of the job involves high-stakes clinical judgment and empathetic, real-time patient care that is difficult to automate.

rows.title: Writers and authors
rows.old_rationale: The core work product of writers and authors is entirely digital and text-based, which aligns perfectly with the primary capabilities of Large Language Models. AI can now generate drafts, conduct research, and perform editing tasks across various formats like blogs, copy, and scripts, significantly increasing productivity while simultaneously threatening to automate many entry-level and routine writing roles.
rows.new_rationale: The work is almost entirely digital, involving the creation and processing of information, which makes it highly susceptible to AI-driven automation. While the role requires human creativity, social perceptiveness, and professional accountability for the final output, a significant portion of the drafting and research process is increasingly routine and amenable to AI assistance.

rows.title: Fundraisers
rows.old_rationale: Fundraising involves significant digital work including donor research, data analysis, and the creation of promotional messaging, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. While major gift solicitation and event management require high-level interpersonal skills and physical presence, the majority of the administrative, analytical, and communication-based tasks are moving toward AI-driven workflows.
rows.new_rationale: Fundraising relies heavily on digital tools for donor management and outreach, but the core of the role is deeply rooted in high-stakes interpersonal relationships, persuasion, and trust-building. While data processing and campaign planning are routine, the success of the role depends on human connection and professional judgment in navigating complex donor interactions and legal compliance.

rows.title: Architects
rows.old_rationale: Architects perform high-level knowledge work that is increasingly digital, utilizing CADD and BIM software which is highly susceptible to AI-driven automation in generative design and technical documentation. While the role requires physical site visits and complex client negotiations, a significant portion of the core work—drafting, specification writing, and regulatory compliance—is moving toward high AI integration.
rows.new_rationale: While architects rely heavily on digital tools like BIM and CADD for documentation and design, the role requires significant physical site visits and real-world coordination. The profession is highly dependent on human interaction for client requirements and negotiation, and carries immense legal and safety accountability that necessitates high-level professional judgment.

rows.title: Editors
rows.old_rationale: Editing is a fully digital occupation centered on language processing, a core strength of Large Language Models. AI can already perform high-level copy editing, fact-checking, and rewriting, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the total number of human editors required for routine content management.
rows.new_rationale: The core work of an editor is almost entirely digital, involving the processing, revising, and management of text and media content. While the role requires significant human interaction for coordination and high-stakes judgment regarding editorial standards and ethics, the routine aspects of proofreading and fact-checking are highly susceptible to automation.

rows.title: Flooring installers and tile and stone setters
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are entirely physical, involving manual dexterity, heavy lifting, and real-time adaptation to irregular physical surfaces in unpredictable environments. While AI might assist with minor administrative tasks or design visualization, it cannot perform the tactile labor of cutting, laying, and finishing materials on-site.
rows.new_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely defined by manual, site-specific physical labor, such as cutting, laying, and finishing flooring materials. While workers perform basic measurements and calculations, the core tasks involve physical manipulation of materials in real-world environments, which is highly resistant to AI automation.

rows.title: Operations research analysts
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital, involving data collection, mathematical modeling, and report writing—all areas where AI and Large Language Models excel. While analysts still provide high-level human judgment and stakeholder persuasion, AI can now automate the core technical tasks of coding analytical tools, mining data, and generating optimization simulations, significantly increasing individual productivity and restructuring the role.
rows.new_rationale: The core work is highly digital and involves structured, repeatable analytical processes, making it a prime candidate for AI-driven automation. However, the role requires significant human interaction to gather requirements and persuade stakeholders, as well as high-stakes judgment in interpreting model outputs for organizational decision-making.

rows.title: Natural sciences managers
rows.old_rationale: This role is predominantly knowledge-based, involving data analysis, budgeting, and technical reporting, all of which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement and automation. While the job requires significant human-centric leadership and physical oversight of laboratories, AI will drastically increase productivity in reviewing research, drafting operational reports, and optimizing resource allocation.
rows.new_rationale: While these managers handle significant digital data and reporting, their role is heavily anchored in the physical reality of laboratory environments and scientific research. The core of the job relies on high-stakes human leadership, team coordination, and expert judgment that is difficult to automate, as they must evaluate complex scientific outcomes and manage personnel.

rows.title: Chemists and materials scientists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a high-level blend of digital knowledge work and physical laboratory experimentation. AI is already revolutionizing the digital aspects—such as molecular modeling, predictive simulation, and technical reporting—allowing scientists to screen millions of compounds in silico before ever entering a lab. While the physical requirement of conducting experiments and handling chemicals provides a buffer, the massive productivity gains in research and analysis mean AI will fundamentally restructure how these scientists spend their time.
rows.new_rationale: While these professionals increasingly use digital modeling and simulation tools, their core work remains deeply rooted in physical laboratory experimentation, material manipulation, and real-world safety testing. The role requires high-stakes professional judgment and accountability for research outcomes and safety standards that are difficult to fully automate or delegate to AI.

rows.title: Probation officers and correctional treatment specialists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of high-exposure digital tasks (writing case reports, risk assessment, and data-driven monitoring) and low-exposure physical/interpersonal tasks (home visits, drug testing, and building trust with hostile clients). While AI can significantly automate documentation and predictive risk modeling, the legal requirement for human testimony and the physical necessity of community supervision provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While this role involves significant documentation and case management, the core of the work is deeply rooted in physical, high-stakes, in-person interactions with clients and the legal system. The necessity for professional judgment, empathy, and accountability in managing human behavior and public safety makes this role highly resistant to automation.

rows.title: Dietitians and nutritionists
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of analyzing nutritional data, interpreting research, and creating personalized meal plans are highly digital and data-driven, making them susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. However, the role requires significant interpersonal counseling, empathy, and clinical judgment in medical settings, which provides a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While dietitians use digital tools for documentation and research, the core of the role is highly dependent on human interaction, empathy, and personalized counseling. The work requires significant professional judgment to translate complex medical and nutritional science into actionable, patient-specific plans, which carries high accountability for health outcomes.

rows.title: Preschool and childcare center directors
rows.old_rationale: This role is a hybrid of administrative knowledge work and high-stakes physical presence. While AI can significantly automate digital tasks like budgeting, scheduling, and curriculum planning, the core of the job involves physical facility oversight, staff supervision, and building trust-based relationships with parents and children that cannot be replicated by AI.
rows.new_rationale: While directors perform administrative and budgeting tasks that can be digitized, the core of the role is deeply embedded in the physical environment of a childcare facility and requires constant, high-stakes human interaction. The necessity for real-time oversight of staff, children, and facility safety, combined with the legal and ethical accountability for child welfare, makes this role highly resistant to AI automation.

rows.title: Interior designers
rows.old_rationale: Interior design is heavily reliant on digital tools like CAD and BIM, which are being rapidly transformed by generative AI capable of creating photorealistic renderings and floor plans from text prompts. While the job requires physical site visits, material inspections, and complex interpersonal coordination with contractors, the core creative and technical drafting tasks are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and productivity gains.
rows.new_rationale: Interior design relies heavily on digital CAD and BIM software for technical documentation, yet the role is fundamentally anchored in physical site visits, material selection, and construction oversight. Success depends on high-stakes interpersonal negotiation with clients and contractors, as well as professional accountability for safety, building codes, and structural feasibility.

rows.title: Veterinarians
rows.old_rationale: Veterinary work is a hybrid of physical procedures and complex diagnostic knowledge work. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic accuracy through image analysis (X-rays/ultrasounds) and treatment planning, the core of the job requires physical surgery, manual examinations, and high-stakes interpersonal communication with pet owners that cannot be automated.
rows.new_rationale: Veterinary medicine is highly dependent on physical interaction with animals, manual dexterity for surgery, and real-world diagnostic procedures that cannot be digitized. While they use medical technology, the core of the role relies on high-stakes clinical judgment and the ability to navigate complex, empathetic relationships with animal owners, making it highly resistant to AI automation.

rows.title: Marriage and family therapists
rows.old_rationale: While the work is increasingly digital via telehealth and involves significant information processing, the core value of therapy lies in human-to-human empathy, trust, and the navigation of complex interpersonal dynamics. AI will likely automate administrative tasks and provide diagnostic support, but the high-stakes emotional nature of family and marriage counseling provides a significant barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is deeply rooted in human empathy, trust, and complex interpersonal dynamics that are difficult to replicate via AI. While administrative tasks and some therapy sessions are increasingly digital, the high-stakes clinical judgment and the requirement for a licensed professional to manage human emotional health make this role highly resistant to automation.

rows.title: Computer hardware engineers
rows.old_rationale: The core work of designing schematics, analyzing circuits, and testing components is increasingly performed using digital Electronic Design Automation (EDA) tools that are highly susceptible to AI-driven optimization and automation. While there is a physical component to testing and manufacturing oversight, the vast majority of the engineering lifecycle is digital and benefits significantly from AI's ability to optimize complex systems and generate code/hardware descriptions.
rows.new_rationale: The work involves high-level digital design and simulation, but remains anchored in the physical world through the testing and manufacturing of tangible hardware components. While AI can assist with routine design and analysis, the role requires significant professional judgment and accountability for complex, high-stakes engineering outcomes and cross-functional team coordination.

rows.title: Interpreters and translators
rows.old_rationale: The core work product is purely digital information (text and speech) in a domain where Large Language Models already demonstrate near-human proficiency. While high-stakes interpreting (legal, medical) and sign language still require human presence and nuanced judgment, the vast majority of translation and routine interpreting tasks are being rapidly automated or shifted to 'post-editing' roles, significantly reducing the total human labor required per unit of work.
rows.new_rationale: The core work of translation is highly digital and involves significant routine information processing, which is increasingly augmented by AI tools. However, interpreting requires high levels of human interaction, cultural nuance, and real-time judgment, particularly in sensitive settings like healthcare or legal proceedings, which limits the extent to which the role can be fully automated.

rows.title: Travel agents
rows.old_rationale: The core functions of a travel agent—researching destinations, comparing prices, building itineraries, and processing bookings—are entirely digital and involve information synthesis that AI can now perform instantly. While high-end human agents provide personalized judgment and crisis management, AI's ability to handle complex natural language queries and real-time logistics makes the vast majority of the occupation's routine tasks highly susceptible to automation.
rows.new_rationale: Travel agents perform the vast majority of their work through digital reservation systems and online information databases, making them highly susceptible to automation. While they rely on human interaction for sales and personalized service, the core tasks of booking, scheduling, and cost calculation are highly routine and information-heavy, which are primary targets for AI-driven platforms.

rows.title: Labor relations specialists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is predominantly digital and knowledge-based, involving the drafting of contracts, policy development, and legal compliance—all areas where AI excels. While the high-stakes interpersonal negotiation and dispute resolution provide a buffer, AI will significantly automate the research, document generation, and investigative analysis components of the role.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the documentation and policy drafting is digital and involves routine processing, the core of the role relies heavily on high-stakes interpersonal negotiation, conflict resolution, and empathy. The work requires significant professional judgment and accountability in interpreting labor laws and managing sensitive disputes, which are difficult to fully automate.

rows.title: Special effects artists and animators
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital, with core tasks like 3D modeling, rendering, and animation being directly disrupted by generative AI and automated rigging tools. While high-level creative direction and complex team collaboration remain human-centric, AI is rapidly automating the 'routine' aspects of CGI and visual effects, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the total number of workers needed for large-scale productions.
rows.new_rationale: The work is almost entirely digital, relying on specialized software and code to produce visual assets. While the creative process involves significant human collaboration and subjective artistic judgment, many technical aspects of animation and rendering are increasingly susceptible to automation and routine algorithmic processing.

rows.title: Sales engineers
rows.old_rationale: Sales engineers perform high-level knowledge work that is increasingly digital, including technical presentations, product customization, and troubleshooting. While AI can automate technical documentation, demo generation, and data-driven customer insights, the role's heavy reliance on complex interpersonal relationships, negotiation, and trust-building provides a significant buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: Sales engineers rely heavily on digital tools for presentations and technical documentation, but their core value lies in high-stakes interpersonal persuasion, negotiation, and complex technical problem-solving. While they handle routine information, the need to customize solutions for specific client needs and manage high-value professional relationships requires significant human judgment and accountability that is difficult to automate.

rows.title: Technical writers
rows.old_rationale: Technical writing is a fully digital occupation centered on synthesizing complex information into clear documentation, a core strength of Large Language Models. AI can already draft manuals, summarize technical specs, and generate code documentation, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the total number of human writers needed for routine documentation tasks.
rows.new_rationale: Technical writing is highly digital and involves significant routine information processing, such as drafting, editing, and standardizing documentation, which are core tasks for AI automation. While the role requires human collaboration with subject matter experts and empathy for the end-user, the primary output is digital content, making it highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains.

rows.title: Judges and hearing officers
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of researching legal issues, evaluating documents, and writing opinions are highly digital and susceptible to AI automation, which can significantly increase productivity and reduce the need for human staff. However, the role requires high-stakes human judgment, presiding over physical courtrooms, and maintaining public trust, which creates a significant barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the work involves reviewing digital documents and legal research, the core of the role requires high-stakes, non-delegable human judgment and the management of complex interpersonal dynamics in a courtroom setting. The physical presence required to preside over hearings and maintain the decorum of the court, combined with the profound ethical and legal accountability of the position, makes it highly resistant to full automation.

rows.title: Private detectives and investigators
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of digital research and physical field work. AI significantly enhances the digital aspects—such as background checks, social media analysis, and report writing—making investigators much more productive, but the core requirements of physical surveillance, in-person interviews, and undercover store work provide a substantial barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the investigative research is digital, the core of the role relies heavily on physical surveillance, in-person interviews, and real-world site-specific activity. The work requires high-stakes legal judgment and accountability, as investigators must navigate complex privacy laws and ensure evidence is admissible, which is difficult to automate.

rows.title: Entertainment and recreation managers
rows.old_rationale: This role involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, such as budgeting, scheduling, and strategic planning, which is highly susceptible to AI optimization. However, the core of the job requires physical presence to inspect facilities, manage real-time emergencies, and provide high-touch interpersonal leadership and customer service in physical environments like parks and fitness centers.
rows.new_rationale: While these managers use digital tools for budgeting and scheduling, the core of the role is highly dependent on physical presence, facility oversight, and real-time interaction with staff and customers. The job requires significant human-centric skills like leadership, conflict resolution, and empathy, as well as high-stakes accountability for safety and operational success that is difficult to automate.

rows.title: Biochemists and biophysicists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is heavily centered on knowledge work, data analysis, and complex modeling, all of which are being revolutionized by AI (e.g., AlphaFold for protein structure prediction). While physical laboratory work and team management provide a buffer, a significant portion of the role involves literature review, grant writing, and data interpretation—tasks where AI can exponentially increase productivity or automate core analytical functions.
rows.new_rationale: While this role involves significant digital data analysis and modeling, the core of the work remains rooted in physical laboratory experimentation and the manipulation of biological materials. The high level of judgment and accountability is driven by the need for scientific rigor, ethical research practices, and the high-stakes nature of developing medical or environmental solutions.

rows.title: Fashion designers
rows.old_rationale: Fashion design is increasingly digital, utilizing CAD and graphics software for sketching, trend analysis, and virtual modeling—all areas where generative AI is rapidly advancing. While the physical aspects of selecting fabrics, creating prototypes, and overseeing production provide a buffer, the core creative and analytical tasks are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and productivity gains.
rows.new_rationale: While fashion designers increasingly rely on CAD and digital tools for visualization, the core of the role remains deeply rooted in the physical manipulation of textiles, fitting prototypes on models, and assessing material quality. The job requires a high degree of subjective creative judgment and interpersonal coordination with manufacturers and creative directors, which limits the extent to which routine information processing can define the role.

rows.title: Materials engineers
rows.old_rationale: Materials engineering is heavily reliant on digital simulation, data analysis, and computational modeling, areas where AI and machine learning are already accelerating material discovery and property prediction. While the role requires physical lab work and manufacturing oversight, a significant portion of the core value—designing alloys, analyzing failures, and writing technical reports—is increasingly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and automation.
rows.new_rationale: Materials engineers rely heavily on digital simulation and computational modeling, but their work is fundamentally anchored in physical testing, manufacturing environments, and real-world material performance. While they use software for analysis, the high-stakes nature of engineering design, safety, and failure analysis requires significant professional judgment and accountability that cannot be fully automated.

rows.title: Bioengineers and biomedical engineers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves heavy digital knowledge work, including software design, statistical modeling, and technical writing, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation and automation. While physical tasks like equipment maintenance and clinical collaboration provide a buffer, the core engineering and research functions are increasingly driven by AI-enhanced simulations and data analysis.
rows.new_rationale: This role involves significant digital work through modeling, simulation, and software design, but it is heavily anchored in the physical world through the creation of medical devices and clinical testing. The high level of judgment and accountability is driven by the safety-critical nature of medical equipment and the complex, non-routine problem-solving required to integrate engineering with biological systems.

rows.title: Landscape architects
rows.old_rationale: Landscape architecture is a predominantly digital knowledge-based occupation where core tasks like CADD modeling, site analysis, and cost estimation are highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. While the role requires physical site visits and interpersonal client management, the generative design capabilities of AI can significantly accelerate the creative and technical drafting phases, potentially reducing the total human hours required per project.
rows.new_rationale: Landscape architects rely heavily on digital design tools (CADD, GIS) for their primary output, but their work is deeply rooted in physical site constraints and environmental variables that require on-site inspection. The role involves significant human interaction for client negotiation and project coordination, alongside high-stakes professional accountability for safety, environmental compliance, and regulatory adherence.

rows.title: Economists
rows.old_rationale: Economists perform work that is almost entirely digital, involving data analysis, mathematical modeling, and technical writing.
