AI is exceptionally proficient at the core tasks of this role, including processing large datasets, identifying statistical patterns, and generating reports, which significantly increases productivity and reduces the number of human workers needed for routine analysis.
rows.new_rationale: Economists perform highly digital, information-intensive work involving statistical modeling and data analysis, which are core areas for AI advancement. However, the role requires significant high-stakes judgment in interpreting complex economic trends and communicating findings to stakeholders, which necessitates human accountability and professional nuance.

rows.title: Nuclear engineers
rows.old_rationale: Nuclear engineering is a high-level knowledge profession where core tasks—such as reactor design, dose calculations, and safety simulations—are performed using digital tools and complex mathematical modeling. While the high-stakes regulatory environment and the need for physical inspections at power plants provide a buffer, AI is rapidly advancing in its ability to optimize designs, analyze sensor data for predictive maintenance, and automate technical documentation.
rows.new_rationale: Nuclear engineering involves high-level digital modeling and simulation, but the work is fundamentally tethered to the physical safety and operation of complex, high-stakes infrastructure. The extreme consequences of failure in nuclear environments necessitate human oversight and professional accountability that are difficult to delegate to AI, despite the routine nature of some data analysis and regulatory documentation.

rows.title: Emergency management directors
rows.old_rationale: The role involves significant digital knowledge work, such as analyzing hazards, drafting complex response plans, and managing federal funding applications, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the core of the job requires high-stakes real-time decision-making, physical presence at command centers during disasters, and intense interpersonal coordination with government officials and the public, providing a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the role involves significant digital planning and documentation, the core of the work is deeply rooted in real-world physical coordination, high-stakes crisis management, and complex interpersonal negotiation. The necessity for human judgment and accountability during unpredictable, life-critical emergencies makes this role highly resistant to full automation.

rows.title: Orthotists and prosthetists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of physical, hands-on work including patient examinations, manual measurements, and the physical fabrication or adjustment of medical devices. While AI and computer-aided design (CAD) can greatly enhance the diagnostic and design phases, the core requirements of real-time human interaction, physical dexterity, and clinical judgment in a healthcare setting provide a strong barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: This role is highly dependent on physical interaction with patients and manual fabrication of custom devices, making it resistant to automation. While digital tools are used for design, the core of the work requires high-stakes clinical judgment, empathy, and physical dexterity that are difficult to replicate via AI.

rows.title: Survey researchers
rows.old_rationale: Survey research is a fundamentally digital knowledge occupation involving background research, survey design, statistical analysis, and report generation—all areas where LLMs and specialized AI tools excel. The BLS already projects a decline in employment due to data mining and big data efficiencies, and the rapid advancement of AI in synthesizing qualitative data and automating complex statistical workflows makes this role highly susceptible to significant restructuring and labor reduction.
rows.new_rationale: The core work of survey researchers is heavily centered on digital data collection, statistical analysis, and software-based reporting. While the role involves some human interaction for focus groups or coordination, the increasing reliance on automated data mining and digital survey platforms makes the occupation highly susceptible to AI-driven automation.

rows.title: Marine engineers and naval architects
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves digital design, complex mathematical modeling, and technical report writing, all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and automation. While physical site visits to shipyards and offshore platforms provide a buffer, the shift toward AI-integrated CAD, automated performance testing, and generative design for hulls and machinery significantly reshapes the workflow.
rows.new_rationale: While the design and documentation aspects of the role are highly digital and involve routine technical processing, the work is fundamentally anchored in the physical world through prototyping, testing, and site-specific construction. The role carries high-stakes accountability for safety and performance, requiring professional judgment that is difficult to delegate to AI.

rows.title: Mining and geological engineers
rows.old_rationale: Mining and geological engineering involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, including mine design, technical reporting, and data analysis, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains. However, the role also requires substantial physical presence for site evaluations, safety inspections, and supervising construction in remote, unpredictable environments, which provides a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While these engineers use digital tools for design and reporting, their work is fundamentally anchored in physical, site-specific environments like mines and quarries where they must manage complex, high-stakes safety and operational variables. The role requires significant professional accountability and judgment that is difficult to automate, as they must interpret unique geological conditions and ensure compliance with safety regulations in unpredictable physical settings.

rows.title: Hand laborers and material movers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are fundamentally physical, involving the manual movement, cleaning, and packing of heavy materials in unpredictable real-world environments. While AI can optimize logistics, routing, and inventory tracking, the physical labor itself is resistant to AI and requires advanced robotics rather than software-based intelligence to automate.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is manual, physical labor involving the movement, cleaning, or packing of tangible goods in real-world environments. While some workers use handheld scanners for basic tracking, the work is overwhelmingly defined by physical exertion and site-specific tasks rather than digital or cognitive information processing.

rows.title: Retail sales workers
rows.old_rationale: Retail sales involve a mix of physical tasks (stocking shelves, demonstrating products) and interpersonal interaction that provides a barrier to full automation. However, AI is increasingly handling the information-heavy aspects of the job, such as personalized product recommendations, answering technical questions via kiosks/mobile apps, and automating inventory management and checkout processes, which reduces the total human labor required per store.
rows.new_rationale: Retail sales is heavily dependent on physical presence for customer interaction, product demonstration, and inventory management. While point-of-sale systems are digital, the core value proposition relies on human-to-human persuasion, empathy, and service, which are difficult to fully automate in a physical retail environment.

rows.title: Registered nurses
rows.old_rationale: Registered nursing is a hybrid role that combines significant physical labor, such as administering treatments and moving patients, with complex interpersonal care and emotional support. While AI will heavily impact the knowledge-work components—such as patient monitoring, diagnostic analysis, and care planning—the core requirement for physical presence and human empathy creates a strong barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While nursing involves digital documentation and monitoring, the core of the profession is deeply rooted in physical patient care, manual procedures, and real-time environmental assessment. The role is highly dependent on complex human interaction, empathy, and high-stakes clinical judgment that requires professional accountability, making it resistant to full automation.

rows.title: Cashiers
rows.old_rationale: While the job has physical components like bagging and stocking, the core function of processing transactions is highly susceptible to automation through computer vision and AI-driven self-checkout systems. The BLS already projects a significant decline in employment due to these technological shifts, as AI can increasingly handle age verification, fraud detection, and customer inquiries.
rows.new_rationale: The role is highly routine and increasingly digital, as evidenced by the widespread adoption of automated point-of-sale and self-checkout systems. However, it remains heavily dependent on physical presence for tasks like bagging, stocking, and age verification, which limits the ability to fully automate the position.

rows.title: General office clerks
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this role—data entry, document formatting, scheduling, and information processing—are almost entirely digital and routine, making them highly susceptible to AI automation. While some physical tasks like handling mail or greeting visitors remain, the vast majority of the workload can be absorbed by AI agents and automated software, as reflected in the BLS's projected decline for the occupation.
rows.new_rationale: The role is heavily focused on routine information processing, data entry, and document management, which are highly susceptible to automation. While there is some requirement for physical presence and basic human interaction, the core tasks are increasingly handled by digital systems and software, leading to the projected decline in the occupation.

rows.title: Software developers, quality assurance analysts, and testers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is fundamentally digital, with core tasks like coding, debugging, and test automation being primary use cases for Large Language Models. While high-level system architecture and complex stakeholder communication provide some insulation, AI is drastically increasing individual productivity, which will likely lead to significant restructuring of entry-level roles and QA functions.
rows.new_rationale: The work is almost entirely digital and involves significant routine information processing, such as coding, testing, and documentation, which are primary targets for AI automation. However, the role requires high-stakes judgment in system architecture and security, as well as essential human collaboration to translate complex user needs into functional software, which limits the extent to which the entire role can be delegated to AI.

rows.title: Assemblers and fabricators
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical manipulation, manual dexterity, and real-time adjustments in a physical environment, which provides a significant buffer against AI. While AI and robotics are increasing efficiency and automating some repetitive tasks, a substantial portion of the work—especially in complex assembly, prototyping, and custom fabrication—still requires human presence and tactile skill.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is manual labor and physical manipulation of components in a manufacturing environment, making it highly dependent on the physical world. While workers interact with digital schematics and automated systems, the primary value is derived from physical assembly, quality control, and dexterity rather than information processing or interpersonal relationships.

rows.title: Construction laborers and helpers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are almost entirely physical, involving manual labor, tool operation, and site preparation in unpredictable outdoor environments. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like scheduling or specialized robotic equipment control, the vast majority of the work requires human presence, physical strength, and real-time adaptation to physical obstacles.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is manual labor performed in physical environments, requiring significant physical strength and stamina. While some tools are becoming digitized, the work is fundamentally defined by site-specific, non-routine physical tasks that are highly resistant to automation.

rows.title: General maintenance and repair workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and real-time problem-solving in unpredictable physical environments, which are currently beyond the reach of AI and robotics. AI may assist with peripheral tasks like diagnosing equipment issues via computer vision or managing supply orders, but the primary work of repairing buildings and machinery remains a hands-on human requirement.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is manual labor, physical repair, and site-specific maintenance, which are highly resistant to automation. While there is some minor digital interaction for record-keeping or ordering supplies, the work is fundamentally defined by physical presence and the use of hand tools in the real world.

rows.title: Bookkeeping, accounting, and auditing clerks
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital and involves routine information processing, data entry, and mathematical verification—tasks that AI and automated software already perform with high accuracy. The BLS already projects a decline in employment due to technological change, and the shift toward 'analytical and advisory' roles is increasingly being encroached upon by advanced LLMs and specialized financial AI agents.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation involves highly structured, digital data entry and reconciliation tasks that are prime candidates for automation. While there is some need for communication and basic professional integrity, the work is predominantly defined by repeatable information processing and digital software interaction.

rows.title: Accountants and auditors
rows.old_rationale: Accountancy is a fundamentally digital occupation centered on data analysis, regulatory compliance, and reporting, all of which are highly susceptible to AI and robotic process automation. While high-level advisory roles and complex forensic audits require human judgment and relationship management, the core tasks of data organization, tax computation, and anomaly detection are increasingly being automated, significantly increasing worker productivity and restructuring the entry-level labor market.
rows.new_rationale: The core work of accountants is highly digital and involves significant routine information processing, making it a prime candidate for AI-driven automation. However, the role remains protected by high requirements for professional judgment, legal accountability, and the need for human-centric advisory and communication skills when interacting with clients and stakeholders.

rows.title: Delivery truck drivers and driver/sales workers
rows.old_rationale: While AI and autonomous vehicle technology are advancing, the core of this job involves significant physical labor, such as loading/unloading cargo and navigating unpredictable 'last-mile' environments. AI may optimize routing and assist with sales communication, but the physical requirement to move goods from a vehicle to a customer's door remains a major barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical labor and vehicle operation, which are highly dependent on the physical world and difficult to automate fully. While digital tools are used for routing and communication, the primary value is the manual movement of goods and face-to-face interaction, limiting the impact of AI on the fundamental tasks.

rows.title: Nursing assistants and orderlies
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of nursing assistants and orderlies—such as bathing, dressing, and physically transferring patients—are highly manual and require real-time human presence in unpredictable physical environments. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like recording vital signs or scheduling, the primary work product is physical care and interpersonal support, which remains largely insulated from AI automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical, hands-on care for patients, which requires constant physical presence and manual manipulation. While there is some documentation and reporting involved, the role is defined by high-touch human interaction, empathy, and physical labor that is largely resistant to digital or AI automation.

rows.title: Security guards and gambling surveillance officers
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical presence and digital monitoring. While AI-powered computer vision is rapidly automating the 'surveillance' and 'monitoring' aspects of the job, the core requirements of physical patrolling, detaining suspects, and responding to real-world emergencies provide a significant barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While surveillance roles are increasingly digital, the core of the profession remains highly dependent on physical presence, patrolling, and real-world intervention. The job requires significant human interaction for de-escalation and high-stakes judgment regarding safety and security, which limits the extent to which AI can fully replace the role.

rows.title: Financial clerks
rows.old_rationale: Financial clerks perform routine, digital-first tasks such as data entry, record updating, and basic financial calculations that are highly susceptible to AI automation. While some roles involve customer interaction, the core functions—processing claims, verifying credit, and managing payroll—are increasingly handled by AI-driven software, leading to a projected decline in employment.
rows.new_rationale: The work of financial clerks is almost entirely centered on digital record-keeping, data entry, and transaction processing, which are highly susceptible to automation. While some roles involve customer interaction, the core tasks are repetitive, rule-based, and rely on standardized procedures rather than high-stakes professional judgment.

rows.title: Management analysts
rows.old_rationale: Management analysts perform high-level knowledge work—including data analysis, report writing, and financial modeling—that is highly susceptible to AI augmentation and automation. While the role requires significant interpersonal skills for client interviews and stakeholder management, the core 'back-office' tasks of synthesizing information and developing efficiency recommendations are increasingly being streamlined by AI tools.
rows.new_rationale: Management analysts rely heavily on digital tools for data analysis and reporting, and much of their work involves structured information processing. However, the role is deeply dependent on high-stakes human interaction, persuasion, and professional judgment to navigate unique organizational challenges that are difficult to fully automate.

rows.title: Project management specialists
rows.old_rationale: Project management is a digital-heavy knowledge occupation involving data analysis, scheduling, and documentation, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation and optimization. While AI can significantly increase productivity in planning and monitoring, the role's heavy reliance on human-centric skills like stakeholder negotiation, conflict resolution, and team leadership provides a protective barrier against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: Project management is highly digital and involves significant routine information processing, such as tracking budgets, schedules, and documentation, which are prime areas for AI augmentation. However, the role is heavily dependent on human relationship management, negotiation, and high-stakes judgment to resolve complex, non-routine interpersonal and project-based conflicts that are difficult to automate.

rows.title: Receptionists
rows.old_rationale: Many core tasks such as scheduling, answering phones, and data entry are highly susceptible to automation via AI voice agents and chatbots. While the role retains a physical component for greeting and security, the BLS already projects zero growth due to automation, and the digital nature of the administrative workload makes it highly exposed to further restructuring.
rows.new_rationale: Receptionists perform highly routine administrative tasks that are increasingly digitized, such as scheduling and data entry, making them prime candidates for automation. While the role requires physical presence for greeting visitors and security, the core information-processing duties are largely repeatable and structured.

rows.title: Metal and plastic machine workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of the work is physical and requires presence on a factory floor to handle materials and maintain machinery. However, AI and advanced automation are increasingly capable of handling the digital aspects of the job, such as CNC programming, machine monitoring, and quality inspection, which is already contributing to a projected decline in employment.
rows.new_rationale: While the work increasingly involves CNC programming and digital interfaces, the core of the occupation remains the physical manipulation, monitoring, and maintenance of heavy industrial machinery. The role is highly dependent on physical presence and manual interaction with materials, with limited reliance on complex human interpersonal relationships.

rows.title: Childcare workers
rows.old_rationale: The core responsibilities of this role—physical care, safety monitoring, hygiene, and emotional support—require a physical presence and human empathy that AI cannot replicate. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like lesson planning, record-keeping, or parent communication, the fundamental nature of the work is hands-on and occurs in the physical world.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical, hands-on care for children, which requires constant physical presence and high-stakes, real-time decision-making regarding safety and development. While there is minor administrative record-keeping, the work is almost entirely non-digital and relies heavily on deep, empathetic human interaction that cannot be automated.

rows.title: Human resources specialists
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of recruiting, screening, and administrative record-keeping are highly digital and data-driven, making them prime targets for AI automation and augmentation. While the role requires significant interpersonal interaction and human judgment for interviewing and employee relations, AI can now handle complex scheduling, initial candidate vetting, and policy inquiries, significantly increasing individual worker productivity and reducing the need for entry-level administrative staff.
rows.new_rationale: The role is highly digital, involving significant routine processing of records, payroll, and applicant data. However, it remains heavily dependent on human interaction for interviewing, conflict resolution, and employee relations, as well as high-stakes judgment regarding hiring and regulatory compliance.

rows.title: Food preparation workers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties are highly physical and require manual dexterity in unpredictable kitchen environments, which are difficult for AI to automate directly. While AI might optimize inventory management or scheduling, the primary tasks of washing, cutting, and preparing food remain resistant to digital replacement, though some labor may be displaced by industrial food processing automation.
rows.new_rationale: The work is almost entirely manual and physical, involving the direct handling of food and kitchen equipment in a high-stakes, real-world environment. While there is some routine task-based coordination with kitchen staff, the role lacks significant digital interaction or complex information processing, making it highly dependent on physical presence and dexterity.

rows.title: Computer support specialists
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of diagnosing technical issues, documenting problems, and guiding users through digital workflows are highly susceptible to AI automation via advanced chatbots and LLM-driven troubleshooting. While some physical hardware setup and complex onsite networking remain, the vast majority of the work is digital information processing that AI can increasingly handle, as evidenced by the BLS's projected decline in employment due to automation.
rows.new_rationale: The work is highly digital and involves significant routine troubleshooting and information processing, making it a prime candidate for AI-driven automation. While some tasks require physical presence for hardware repair or interpersonal skills for customer support, the core diagnostic and documentation aspects are increasingly handled by automated systems.

rows.title: Material moving machine operators
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job is physical operation of heavy machinery in dynamic environments like warehouses and construction sites, which provides a buffer against full automation. However, AI-driven computer vision and autonomous navigation are increasingly being integrated into forklifts and conveyor systems, leading to moderate exposure as these technologies augment or replace human operators in structured settings.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is the physical manipulation of heavy machinery in real-world environments like construction sites and warehouses. While there is some digital integration for tracking and monitoring, the work is fundamentally defined by manual control, spatial awareness, and physical safety, which are difficult to fully automate or digitize.

rows.title: Physicians and surgeons
rows.old_rationale: Physicians and surgeons have moderate exposure because while AI can significantly automate digital tasks like diagnostic image interpretation (radiology), medical history analysis, and treatment planning, the core of the job requires physical presence, manual dexterity for procedures, and complex interpersonal communication. The high-stakes nature of medical liability and the need for human empathy in patient care provide a natural barrier to full automation, though AI will drastically increase productivity in information-heavy specialties.
rows.new_rationale: While physicians increasingly rely on digital health records and diagnostic imaging, the core of the profession remains deeply rooted in physical examinations, surgical procedures, and high-stakes, real-world patient interaction. The role requires profound human empathy, complex physical dexterity, and ultimate professional accountability for life-and-death decisions that cannot be delegated to automated systems.

rows.title: Farmers, ranchers, and other agricultural managers
rows.old_rationale: The role involves a significant amount of physical labor, equipment maintenance, and real-time management of biological systems that cannot be digitized. However, AI will heavily impact the management side of the job, including crop yield prediction, financial planning, and automated resource allocation, making individual managers significantly more productive and contributing to further farm consolidation.
rows.new_rationale: While these managers increasingly use digital tools for market analysis and record-keeping, the core of the occupation remains deeply rooted in physical, site-specific work involving biological systems and machinery. The role requires high-stakes, autonomous decision-making regarding unpredictable environmental and market variables that are difficult to fully automate or delegate.

rows.title: Police and detectives
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job involves physical presence, emergency response, and manual tasks like making arrests and patrolling, which are resistant to AI. However, a significant portion of the work involves digital tasks such as writing detailed reports, searching databases, and analyzing evidence, all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and efficiency gains.
rows.new_rationale: While police work involves digital record-keeping and database lookups, the core of the profession is defined by physical presence, real-time intervention, and high-stakes decision-making in unpredictable environments. The reliance on human empathy, negotiation, and legal accountability makes the role highly resistant to automation, as these tasks require complex moral and situational judgment that cannot be delegated to AI.

rows.title: Electricians
rows.old_rationale: The core work of an electrician is highly physical, involving manual installation, repair, and navigation of complex, unpredictable physical environments like construction sites and crawl spaces. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like blueprint analysis, diagnostic calculations, or scheduling, it cannot perform the tactile labor or real-time physical problem-solving required for the job.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly manual and physical, requiring on-site manipulation of electrical systems in diverse, often challenging environments. While they use digital diagnostic tools and read technical diagrams, the work is fundamentally defined by physical labor and high-stakes safety accountability that is difficult to automate or delegate to AI.

rows.title: Medical assistants
rows.old_rationale: Medical assistants perform a hybrid of physical and digital tasks; while AI can significantly automate administrative duties like scheduling, coding, and medical record entry, it cannot perform physical clinical tasks such as drawing blood, administering injections, or taking manual vital signs. The role requires a high degree of real-time human interaction and empathy in a physical clinical setting, which serves as a natural barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: Medical assistants perform a hybrid role that combines significant routine administrative data entry with essential, high-touch physical tasks like taking vitals and drawing blood. While the administrative portion is highly susceptible to digital automation, the core clinical duties require physical presence, empathy, and direct patient interaction that are difficult to replace with AI.

rows.title: Social workers
rows.old_rationale: Social work involves a high degree of interpersonal interaction, empathy, and crisis management in physical environments, which provides a strong barrier against full automation. However, AI will significantly impact the profession by automating administrative tasks like case documentation, assisting in resource research, and providing diagnostic support tools for clinical practitioners.
rows.new_rationale: Social work is fundamentally centered on high-stakes human interaction, empathy, and complex ethical judgment that requires physical presence and real-world intervention. While there is a digital component for documentation and record-keeping, the core of the profession relies on interpersonal trust, crisis response, and professional accountability that is difficult to automate.

rows.title: Automotive service technicians and mechanics
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and real-time mechanical repair in a physical environment, which provides a strong barrier against AI automation. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic capabilities and predictive maintenance software, the actual physical replacement of parts and hands-on troubleshooting remains a human-centric task.
rows.new_rationale: While automotive technicians increasingly use computerized diagnostic tools, the core of the job remains highly physical, requiring manual manipulation of heavy machinery and complex mechanical systems. The work involves significant accountability for safety-critical repairs, but it is fundamentally grounded in the physical world rather than digital information processing.

rows.title: Computer and information systems managers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is predominantly digital and knowledge-based, involving strategic planning, analysis, and technical oversight that AI can significantly augment. While high-level leadership, vendor negotiation, and personnel management provide a buffer of human judgment, the core tasks of assessing IT needs, monitoring security, and directing technical workflows are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and automation.
rows.new_rationale: The role is entirely centered on digital infrastructure and information systems, making it highly exposed to digital transformation. However, the core responsibilities involve high-stakes strategic decision-making, personnel management, and complex stakeholder negotiation that require significant human judgment and accountability, which are difficult to fully automate.

rows.title: Sales managers
rows.old_rationale: Sales managers perform high-level knowledge work including data analysis, sales forecasting, and strategic planning, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. While the role requires significant interpersonal leadership and relationship management, much of the core digital workflow—such as monitoring CRM data, generating reports, and developing training materials—is being rapidly transformed by generative and analytical AI.
rows.new_rationale: Sales managers rely heavily on digital CRM and data analysis tools for routine forecasting and performance tracking. However, the core of the role is deeply rooted in high-stakes human interaction, leadership, and strategic decision-making that requires nuanced judgment and interpersonal influence.

rows.title: Medical and health services managers
rows.old_rationale: This role involves a high volume of digital knowledge work, such as budgeting, scheduling, and regulatory compliance, which are highly susceptible to AI optimization. However, the core responsibilities of recruiting, supervising staff, and navigating complex interpersonal relationships within a physical healthcare facility provide a significant buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While these managers rely heavily on digital systems for billing, scheduling, and health records, their core work is deeply embedded in the physical healthcare environment and requires high-stakes human interaction. The role demands significant professional judgment and accountability for regulatory compliance, personnel management, and facility operations, which are difficult to fully automate.

rows.title: Purchasing managers, buyers, and purchasing agents
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of analyzing price proposals, monitoring inventory, and reviewing financial reports are highly susceptible to AI automation and optimization. While high-stakes negotiations and physical site visits provide a buffer, the majority of the work is digital information processing that can be significantly streamlined by AI, leading to higher productivity per worker and a restructuring of the procurement process.
rows.new_rationale: The role involves significant digital information processing, such as analyzing financial reports and managing inventory records, which are highly susceptible to automation. However, the core of the profession relies heavily on high-stakes human interaction, including complex contract negotiations, relationship management with vendors, and strategic decision-making that requires professional accountability.

rows.title: Insurance sales agents
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of analyzing policies, customizing insurance programs, and maintaining records are digital and data-driven, making them highly susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. While the role requires interpersonal trust and persuasive sales skills, AI-powered chatbots and recommendation engines are increasingly capable of handling routine inquiries and policy comparisons, significantly increasing individual agent productivity and reducing the need for human intervention in simple transactions.
rows.new_rationale: The role is highly digital and involves significant routine information processing, such as analyzing policies and managing records, which are prime targets for automation. However, the core of the job relies heavily on human relationship-building, persuasion, and trust-based advisory services, which are difficult for AI to replicate fully.

rows.title: Preschool teachers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical care, emotional support, and real-time social interaction with children under age five, which AI cannot replicate. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like lesson planning, curriculum design, and progress reporting, the primary job requires physical presence and human empathy to manage safety and developmental needs.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is physical, in-person interaction with young children, which cannot be replaced by digital tools. While there is some routine documentation, the primary work involves high-stakes, real-time emotional and developmental engagement that requires human presence and professional judgment.

rows.title: Construction managers
rows.old_rationale: Construction management is a hybrid role that combines digital knowledge work—such as cost estimation, scheduling, and contract analysis—with essential physical presence. While AI can significantly automate budgeting and logistical planning, the core of the job requires real-world site inspections, physical problem-solving, and high-stakes interpersonal negotiation with tradesworkers and inspectors that cannot be replicated digitally.
rows.new_rationale: While construction managers use digital tools for budgeting and scheduling, the core of the role is highly dependent on physical presence at worksites and the management of complex, real-world variables. The job requires significant human interaction for coordination and high-stakes judgment to handle emergencies, safety compliance, and project-critical decision-making that cannot be easily automated.

rows.title: Computer systems analysts
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is fundamentally digital, involving data modeling, system design, and technical analysis that can be performed entirely on a computer. AI is highly capable of automating core tasks such as writing technical documentation, generating system diagrams, analyzing cost-benefit data, and assisting with the programming and debugging aspects of the role.
rows.new_rationale: The work is almost entirely digital, involving the design and analysis of complex IT systems. While it involves significant routine information processing and data modeling, it is highly dependent on human interaction to bridge the gap between business needs and technical implementation, and requires high-stakes judgment to ensure organizational efficiency and system reliability.

rows.title: Welders, cutters, solderers, and brazers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, requiring manual dexterity, physical stamina, and real-time adaptation to unpredictable environments like construction sites or repair shops. While AI and robotics can automate routine assembly-line welding, the specialized, non-routine tasks and the need for physical presence in varied locations provide a strong barrier against AI displacement.
rows.new_rationale: This occupation is fundamentally defined by manual, physical interaction with materials in the real world, requiring high levels of dexterity and physical stamina. While workers must interpret blueprints and exercise judgment regarding heat and material integrity, the core tasks are not digital or information-processing heavy, and the role involves minimal interpersonal dependency.

rows.title: Training and development specialists
rows.old_rationale: Much of the core work, such as designing training manuals, creating online learning modules, and assessing needs through data, is digital and highly susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. While the role requires interpersonal skills for live delivery and collaboration, AI can now generate curriculum, simulate training scenarios, and personalize learning paths, significantly increasing individual productivity and potentially reducing the number of specialists needed for content creation.
rows.new_rationale: The role involves significant digital creation of learning modules and administrative data management, but it is heavily anchored in human interaction, facilitation, and the nuanced assessment of organizational needs. While some tasks are routine, the core requirement to adapt training to diverse audiences and manage interpersonal dynamics requires a high degree of human-centric judgment that is difficult to fully automate.

rows.title: Social and human service assistants
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of digital administrative work and high-touch interpersonal service. AI can significantly automate the 'knowledge work' components, such as researching community resources, assisting with complex benefit applications, and coordinating service schedules. However, the core of the job requires physical presence in homes or clinics and deep emotional intelligence to support clients in crisis, which provides a substantial buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the role involves significant administrative paperwork and digital record-keeping, the core of the work is deeply rooted in face-to-face human interaction, empathy, and physical presence in the community. The high-stakes nature of assessing client needs and ensuring safety in sensitive situations requires professional judgment that is difficult to automate, despite the routine aspects of navigating social service systems.

rows.title: Animal care and service workers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are physical and require real-time presence, such as feeding, grooming, cleaning, and physically training animals. AI may assist with peripheral administrative tasks like scheduling or monitoring animal health data, but it cannot replace the manual labor or the empathetic human-animal interaction required for the job.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical interaction with animals, requiring manual labor, direct observation, and site-specific care that cannot be digitized or automated. While there is some administrative record-keeping and client communication, the primary value is derived from the physical presence and specialized handling of living creatures.

rows.title: Heating, air conditioning, and refrigeration mechanics and installers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and real-time problem solving in unpredictable physical environments, which are highly resistant to AI. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like diagnostic software, scheduling, or energy load calculations, it cannot perform the physical installation, repair, or handling of hazardous materials required for the job.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is manual, site-specific physical labor involving the installation and repair of mechanical systems, which is highly resistant to automation. While technicians use digital tools for diagnostics and record-keeping, the primary work product is physical maintenance and repair that requires human judgment and physical presence in varied, often difficult environments.

rows.title: Compliance officers
rows.old_rationale: Compliance work is primarily digital and involves analyzing vast amounts of regulatory text, auditing data, and generating reports—all areas where LLMs and automated systems excel. While high-level judgment and interpersonal investigations provide some insulation, the core tasks of monitoring, risk assessment, and documentation are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and productivity gains.
rows.new_rationale: Compliance work is highly digital and involves significant routine information processing, such as auditing, documentation, and regulatory lookup, making it a prime candidate for AI augmentation. However, the role requires high-stakes professional judgment, accountability for legal outcomes, and interpersonal skills for training and investigation, which limits full automation.

rows.title: Correctional officers and bailiffs
rows.old_rationale: The core duties are physical and interpersonal, requiring real-time human presence to maintain security, conduct searches, and physically restrain individuals. AI exposure is limited to peripheral administrative tasks like report writing and log entry, or enhancing surveillance systems, but it cannot replace the physical oversight and intervention required in correctional and courtroom environments.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical presence, security, and the immediate, high-stakes management of human behavior in volatile environments. While there is some routine documentation, the job is fundamentally defined by physical intervention, real-time situational awareness, and the exercise of professional judgment that cannot be delegated to digital systems.

rows.title: Paralegals and legal assistants
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation—legal research, document drafting, and information organization—are fundamentally digital and align perfectly with the strengths of Large Language Models. While some interpersonal and courtroom logistics remain, AI can already automate or significantly accelerate the vast majority of a paralegal's workload, leading to the BLS's projection of 0% job growth despite high demand for legal services.
rows.new_rationale: The core tasks of paralegals—legal research, document drafting, and file management—are highly digital and involve significant routine information processing, making them prime candidates for AI-driven efficiency. While the role requires interpersonal coordination and professional judgment in supporting attorneys, the majority of the daily output is information-based and document-centric.

rows.title: Fitness trainers and instructors
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation requires physical presence to demonstrate exercises, correct form in real-time to prevent injury, and provide high-touch motivational support. While AI can automate peripheral tasks like workout programming, nutrition planning, and social media marketing, it cannot replace the physical safety monitoring and interpersonal accountability that define the role.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is physical instruction, real-time observation of movement, and high-touch interpersonal motivation, which are difficult to replicate via AI. While digital tools are used for scheduling and marketing, the primary value proposition is the physical presence and the human-to-human relationship required to ensure safety and client success.

rows.title: Civil engineers
rows.old_rationale: Civil engineering involves significant digital knowledge work, including CAD design, data analysis, and cost estimation, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains. However, the role is anchored by the necessity for physical site inspections, real-world surveying, and the legal accountability of a Professional Engineer (PE) license, which requires human judgment and liability that AI cannot currently replace.
rows.new_rationale: Civil engineering relies heavily on digital design software and data analysis, but the work is fundamentally anchored in physical construction sites and real-world environmental constraints. While routine tasks like drafting and cost estimation are increasingly automated, the role requires high-stakes professional judgment and legal accountability for public safety, which limits the extent to which AI can fully replace the human engineer.

rows.title: Claims adjusters, appraisers, examiners, and investigators
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is heavily focused on information processing, document review, and data-driven decision-making, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation. While physical inspections and interpersonal negotiations provide some protection, the BLS already projects a decline in employment due to AI's ability to analyze photos of damage and automate claim calculations.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role involves processing structured information, reviewing documentation, and applying policy guidelines, which are highly susceptible to automation. While the job requires physical site visits and interpersonal negotiation, the analytical and documentation-heavy components are increasingly being handled by AI-driven software and image recognition tools.

rows.title: Machinists and tool and die makers
rows.old_rationale: The role involves a significant physical component, including manual dexterity, machine setup, and material handling in a factory environment. However, AI and advanced automation are increasingly capable of optimizing CNC programming, interpreting CAD designs, and monitoring machine health, which increases worker productivity and reduces the demand for traditional tool and die making skills.
rows.new_rationale: While the role increasingly relies on digital CAD/CAM and CNC programming, the core output is the physical manipulation of materials, requiring significant manual dexterity and site-specific machine operation. The work involves high-stakes precision and accountability for the integrity of the manufactured parts, but it is largely independent of interpersonal relationships.

rows.title: Food service managers
rows.old_rationale: While AI can significantly automate administrative tasks like staff scheduling, inventory ordering, and payroll management, the core of the job requires physical presence and real-time human interaction. Managers must physically inspect facilities for safety, handle unpredictable interpersonal conflicts with staff and customers, and perform manual labor during peak hours, which provides a strong buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While food service managers handle some digital administrative tasks like payroll and scheduling, the core of their role is highly dependent on physical presence, site-specific operations, and real-time management of staff and customers. The job requires significant interpersonal skills for conflict resolution and leadership, as well as high-stakes accountability for safety, sanitation, and business performance in a dynamic, non-digital environment.

rows.title: Industrial engineers
rows.old_rationale: Industrial engineering is heavily focused on data analysis, systems design, and process optimization—tasks where AI and machine learning excel. While the role requires physical site visits and human collaboration to understand workflows, the core work product is digital analysis and modeling, making the occupation highly susceptible to significant productivity gains and restructuring through AI-driven automation.
rows.new_rationale: Industrial engineers rely heavily on digital tools for data analysis, simulation, and design, but their work is deeply rooted in physical-world optimization, requiring on-site observation and interaction with machinery. While much of their data processing is structured, the role requires high-stakes professional judgment to ensure safety, efficiency, and quality, alongside significant interpersonal collaboration to implement changes across organizational departments.

rows.title: Firefighters
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of firefighting are intensely physical and require real-time presence in unpredictable, hazardous environments where AI cannot currently operate. While AI may enhance peripheral tasks like incident reporting, equipment maintenance scheduling, and predictive wildfire modeling, the essential work of rescuing victims and extinguishing fires remains a human-centric manual task.
rows.new_rationale: The core work of a firefighter is fundamentally physical, involving high-stakes, real-time intervention in unpredictable environments that cannot be digitized or automated. While they perform some routine documentation, the primary requirements of the role—emergency response, physical rescue, and life-saving medical care—rely on human judgment, empathy, and physical presence.

rows.title: Recreation workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical presence, manual instruction, and real-time interpersonal interaction in unpredictable environments like camps and community centers. While AI can assist with peripheral administrative tasks like scheduling, program planning, and creating newsletters, it cannot replace the physical supervision, safety monitoring, and emotional leadership required to manage group activities.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is physical, in-person interaction, instruction, and supervision of recreational activities, which requires a high degree of human engagement and physical presence. While there are minor administrative tasks like paperwork or scheduling, these are peripheral to the primary work of leading groups and ensuring participant safety in real-world environments.

rows.title: Personal financial advisors
rows.old_rationale: The core technical tasks of this role—analyzing market data, optimizing portfolios, and tax planning—are digital and highly susceptible to AI automation, as evidenced by the rise of robo-advisors. However, the occupation is protected by a significant interpersonal component, as clients often require human trust, emotional reassurance during market volatility, and complex ethical judgment that AI cannot yet fully replicate.
rows.new_rationale: While the core analytical and data-driven aspects of the job are highly digital and susceptible to automation, the role is fundamentally anchored in high-stakes, trust-based human relationships and personalized advisory services. The need for empathy, persuasion, and professional accountability for a client's financial future makes it difficult to fully delegate the role to AI.

rows.title: Diesel service technicians and mechanics
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, requiring manual dexterity, strength, and real-time problem-solving in a hands-on environment. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic software and technical manuals used by technicians, it cannot perform the physical repairs, parts replacement, or tool manipulation that define the job.
rows.new_rationale: While the role increasingly incorporates digital diagnostic tools and electronic systems, the core of the work remains highly physical, involving manual labor, heavy lifting, and the mechanical repair of tangible equipment. The job requires significant professional judgment to diagnose complex mechanical issues and ensure safety, but it is fundamentally anchored in the physical world rather than digital information processing.

rows.title: Loan officers
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of loan officers—analyzing financial data, verifying documentation, and assessing risk—are fundamentally digital and highly susceptible to AI automation. While complex commercial loans and relationship-based mortgage sales still require human judgment and interpersonal skills, the rapid advancement of automated underwriting and AI-driven customer service significantly reduces the need for human intervention in routine loan processing.
rows.new_rationale: The core work of a loan officer is heavily reliant on digital financial data and automated underwriting software, making it highly susceptible to digital processing. While the role requires significant human interaction for sales and customer guidance, as well as professional judgment for final approvals, the underlying analytical tasks are increasingly standardized and automated.

rows.title: Masonry workers
rows.old_rationale: Masonry is a highly physical trade performed in unpredictable outdoor environments that require manual dexterity, strength, and real-time sensory feedback. While AI and robotics may assist with blueprint analysis or specialized brick-laying machines in controlled settings, the core tasks of navigating muddy sites, handling heavy materials, and finishing surfaces remain resistant to digital automation.
rows.new_rationale: Masonry is a highly manual, physical trade that requires on-site presence and dexterous manipulation of heavy materials, making it largely immune to digital automation. While workers must interpret blueprints and exercise professional judgment regarding structural integrity and material application, the core of the work is inherently tied to the physical environment.

rows.title: EMTs and paramedics
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical intervention, manual dexterity, and real-time human presence in unpredictable environments, which are highly resistant to AI. While AI will significantly assist with peripheral tasks like EKG interpretation, diagnostic decision support, and automated documentation, the physical requirements of patient transport and hands-on emergency care remain essential human functions.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is high-stakes, real-time physical intervention and patient care in unpredictable environments, which is inherently resistant to automation. While documentation is digital, the primary value lies in physical presence, empathetic human interaction, and critical, life-or-death judgment that cannot be delegated to AI.

rows.title: Food processing equipment workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, such as loading ingredients, cleaning machinery, and manual quality checks, which are resistant to AI. While AI can optimize recipes, monitor sensor data, and automate some equipment controls, the requirement for human presence in a physical manufacturing environment to handle unpredictable mechanical issues and sanitation limits overall exposure.
rows.new_rationale: This role is heavily centered on physical labor, manual machine operation, and direct interaction with food products in a manufacturing environment. While there is some routine monitoring of gauges and data recording, the core tasks are physical and site-specific, requiring little interpersonal interaction or high-stakes professional judgment.

rows.title: Physical therapists
rows.old_rationale: The core of physical therapy involves manual manipulation, physical assistance, and real-time observation of human movement in a physical environment, which AI cannot replicate. While AI can assist with administrative tasks, medical history review, and generating personalized exercise plans, the high level of physical dexterity and interpersonal empathy required limits its overall exposure.
rows.new_rationale: Physical therapy is fundamentally defined by hands-on, physical interaction with patients and the requirement for real-time, high-stakes clinical judgment. While digital tools are used for documentation, the core value of the profession relies on human empathy, physical manipulation, and professional accountability that cannot be automated.

rows.title: Bakers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of a baker are physical and sensory, involving manual dexterity for shaping dough and real-time monitoring of physical products in high-heat environments. While AI can optimize recipes or assist with inventory management and scheduling, the fundamental work of kneading, decorating, and handling equipment remains a physical task resistant to digital automation.
rows.new_rationale: Baking is a highly manual, physical occupation centered on the transformation of raw ingredients into food products in a real-world environment. While some bakers manage business operations or interact with customers, the core of the role involves physical labor, sensory evaluation of products, and manual manipulation of materials that are largely resistant to digital or AI-driven automation.

rows.title: Data scientists
rows.old_rationale: Data science is a fully digital occupation centered on coding, statistical modeling, and data analysis—all areas where AI is rapidly achieving parity or superiority. While human judgment is still needed for business context and ethical oversight, AI can now automate significant portions of the data pipeline, including cleaning raw data, generating complex code, and even suggesting optimal model architectures.
rows.new_rationale: Data science is a highly digital profession centered on the manipulation and analysis of information, making it a primary target for AI-driven automation of routine tasks like data cleaning and model building. However, the role retains significant human-centric requirements for interpreting findings, communicating complex insights to stakeholders, and exercising professional judgment in business decision-making.

rows.title: Heavy vehicle and mobile equipment service technicians
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, involving manual dexterity, strength, and real-world troubleshooting in unpredictable environments like farms and construction sites. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic software and predictive maintenance logs, it cannot perform the physical disassembly, repair, and reassembly of heavy mechanical components.
rows.new_rationale: While these technicians increasingly use computerized diagnostic tools, the core of the work is highly physical, involving manual labor, heavy lifting, and site-specific mechanical repair. The role requires significant professional judgment to diagnose complex machinery, but it remains largely insulated from AI due to the necessity of physical interaction with hardware in unpredictable, real-world environments.

rows.title: Logisticians
rows.old_rationale: Logisticians perform high-level knowledge work that is primarily digital, involving data analysis, forecasting, and supply chain optimization—tasks where AI excels. While the role requires human-to-human negotiation and real-world problem-solving for physical disruptions, the core functions of inventory management and route optimization are increasingly being automated or enhanced by sophisticated AI algorithms.
rows.new_rationale: Logisticians rely heavily on digital systems for data management and supply chain tracking, making their work highly digital and information-intensive. However, the role requires significant human coordination with suppliers and customers, as well as physical-world oversight of inventory and distribution, which limits the extent to which AI can fully automate the position.

rows.title: Career and technical education teachers
rows.old_rationale: While AI can significantly automate lesson planning, grading, and the delivery of theoretical knowledge, the core of this occupation involves supervising hands-on activities in physical environments like workshops and labs. Teaching students to safely use physical tools for trades like welding, culinary arts, or auto repair requires real-time human presence and physical intervention that AI cannot currently replicate.
rows.new_rationale: While CTE teachers use digital tools for lesson planning and administration, the core of their work is highly physical, involving hands-on instruction, equipment supervision, and laboratory management. The role is deeply dependent on human interaction, mentorship, and high-stakes judgment regarding student safety and professional skill development, which are difficult to automate.

rows.title: Postsecondary education administrators
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is heavily centered on digital information processing, including reviewing applications, analyzing student data, managing budgets, and maintaining academic records—all tasks where AI can significantly increase productivity or automate routine workflows. While roles like deans and student affairs officers require high-level human judgment and interpersonal relationship management, the underlying administrative and analytical work is highly susceptible to AI integration and restructuring.
rows.new_rationale: The role relies heavily on digital systems for record-keeping, scheduling, and data analysis, which are highly susceptible to automation. However, the core of the profession involves significant human-centric tasks such as student advising, faculty management, and high-stakes institutional decision-making that require empathy, trust, and professional accountability.

rows.title: Human resources managers
rows.old_rationale: Human resources management is a digital-heavy knowledge occupation where AI can significantly automate recruitment screening, payroll processing, and regulatory compliance reporting. While the role requires high-level interpersonal skills for dispute mediation and strategic leadership, the vast majority of the administrative and analytical tasks are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and restructuring.
rows.new_rationale: HR management relies heavily on digital systems for data and administration, and involves significant routine information processing regarding policies and compliance. However, the core of the role is deeply rooted in high-stakes human interaction, conflict resolution, and strategic judgment that requires empathy and professional accountability, making it difficult to fully automate.

rows.title: Cost estimators
rows.old_rationale: Cost estimation is a data-intensive, digital-first occupation centered on analyzing blueprints, technical documents, and historical databases to predict costs. AI and advanced software are already driving a projected decline in employment by automating routine calculations and simulations, though human oversight remains necessary for complex site-specific variables and high-stakes negotiations.
rows.new_rationale: Cost estimators rely heavily on digital software, databases, and technical documentation to perform structured analytical tasks, making them highly susceptible to automation. While they require human collaboration and professional judgment to interpret complex project variables, the core of their work involves repeatable data processing that is increasingly handled by specialized modeling tools.

rows.title: Transportation, storage, and distribution managers
rows.old_rationale: This role involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, such as logistics planning, budgeting, and supply chain optimization, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven efficiency gains. However, the core job also requires physical presence for facility inspections, safety compliance monitoring, and high-stakes interpersonal leadership in unpredictable environments like warehouses and railyards, providing a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: This role relies heavily on digital supply chain and logistics management systems, yet it remains deeply anchored in physical operations like warehouses and transportation networks. Success requires a balance of high-stakes operational judgment and the ability to manage complex interpersonal relationships with staff, suppliers, and clients.

rows.title: Web developers and digital designers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is entirely digital, with core tasks like writing code (HTML, JavaScript), designing layouts, and creating prototypes being areas where AI has demonstrated high proficiency. AI tools can now generate functional code from natural language and create sophisticated visual mockups, significantly increasing individual productivity and potentially reducing the number of developers needed for standard projects.
rows.new_rationale: The work is almost entirely digital, involving significant routine information processing such as coding, debugging, and interface design, which are highly susceptible to AI automation. While there is a need for human coordination with clients and team members, the core technical output is increasingly generated or assisted by AI tools, though professional judgment remains necessary for complex system architecture and final quality assurance.

rows.title: Woodworkers
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job involves physical manipulation of materials, manual dexterity, and real-time sensory monitoring in a workshop environment, which are resistant to AI. While AI can optimize digital design (CAD) and CNC programming, the physical assembly, finishing, and machine maintenance require a human presence that limits overall exposure.
rows.new_rationale: While woodworkers increasingly use CNC machines and digital schematics, the core of the job remains the physical manipulation of materials and operation of heavy machinery in a workshop environment. The work is highly manual and task-oriented, requiring physical presence and dexterity rather than interpersonal interaction or high-stakes abstract decision-making.

rows.title: Chefs and head cooks
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job is highly physical and sensory, requiring manual dexterity, real-time human leadership in a fast-paced kitchen, and the use of taste and smell. While AI can significantly assist with peripheral digital tasks like menu planning, inventory management, and recipe generation, it cannot replace the physical oversight and hands-on food preparation essential to the role.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly dependent on physical presence, manual dexterity, and sensory evaluation of food in a real-world kitchen environment. While administrative tasks involve some digital tools and routine processing, the primary value is derived from leadership, creative culinary judgment, and the physical coordination of staff and ingredients.

rows.title: Medical records specialists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital, involving the processing, classification, and entry of data into electronic systems. AI is already highly capable of natural language processing and automated medical coding, which directly automates the core tasks of reviewing records and assigning clinical codes, significantly reducing the need for human intervention.
rows.new_rationale: The role is highly digital and centered on routine information processing, specifically the classification and entry of medical data into electronic systems. While there is a need for interpersonal communication to clarify records and a requirement for professional integrity regarding privacy, the core tasks are heavily susceptible to automation via AI-driven coding and data management tools.

rows.title: Automotive body and glass repairers
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks are highly physical, involving manual dexterity, welding, and the use of pneumatic tools in unpredictable repair environments that are difficult to automate. AI's primary impact is limited to peripheral administrative tasks like cost estimation and damage assessment via computer vision, but the physical restoration of vehicles remains a human-centric trade.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is manual, physical labor involving the repair and restoration of vehicle structures, which is highly resistant to automation. While some information processing occurs during damage assessment and estimation, the primary value is derived from physical dexterity and mechanical expertise in a real-world environment.

rows.title: Drafters
rows.old_rationale: Drafters perform work that is almost entirely digital, converting conceptual sketches into technical CAD and BIM models. AI is rapidly advancing in generative design and automated technical drawing, allowing for the immediate translation of engineering specifications into precise models, which significantly reduces the human labor required for these tasks.
rows.new_rationale: Drafters operate almost entirely within digital CAD and BIM environments, performing highly structured tasks that involve translating specifications into technical drawings. While they collaborate with engineers and occasionally visit sites, their core output is digital information, making them highly susceptible to automation through generative design and AI-assisted modeling.

rows.title: Psychiatric technicians and aides
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical tasks such as bathing, feeding, and physically restraining patients, which AI cannot perform. While AI can assist with monitoring patient behavior and automating documentation or reporting, the high requirement for real-time human presence, physical stamina, and interpersonal compassion creates a significant barrier to automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is physical, hands-on patient care and safety monitoring, which requires a constant physical presence and cannot be digitized. While there is some documentation involved, the primary value is derived from high-stakes interpersonal interaction, empathy, and the physical ability to manage and restrain patients in a clinical environment.

rows.title: Painting and coating workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and presence in industrial or repair environments, which provides a natural barrier to AI. While AI and computer vision can enhance automated spray systems and quality control sensors, the need for human workers to set up equipment, prepare surfaces, and perform manual touch-ups in unpredictable repair settings remains significant.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is manual labor and the operation of physical machinery to treat tangible products, making it highly dependent on the physical world. While some workers interact with computer-controlled systems, the work is primarily tactile and sensory, requiring minimal interpersonal interaction or complex, high-stakes decision-making that would be susceptible to AI automation.

rows.title: Musicians and singers
rows.old_rationale: While AI can generate high-quality digital music and vocals, a significant portion of this occupation involves live performance, physical presence at venues, and human-centric events like religious services and concerts. The 'human element' and the physical act of playing an instrument or singing in real-time provide a natural barrier, though AI will increasingly compete for studio/session work and background music production.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is the physical performance of music, which requires manual dexterity, physical presence, and human-to-human connection with audiences and collaborators. While digital tools are used for promotion and recording, the essential work product—the live musical experience—is inherently physical and relies on human artistry rather than routine information processing.

rows.title: Bill and account collectors
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital, consisting of data analysis, skip tracing, and communication via phone or computer. AI and automated systems are already driving a projected 10% decline in employment by handling routine notifications and payment processing, while advanced LLMs are increasingly capable of managing the negotiation and customer service aspects of debt collection.
rows.new_rationale: The role is highly digital and process-oriented, relying heavily on databases, automated calling systems, and structured record-keeping. While the job requires interpersonal skills for negotiation and empathy, the core tasks are increasingly susceptible to automation and AI-driven communication tools.

rows.title: Occupational health and safety specialists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical fieldwork and digital knowledge work. While AI can significantly automate data analysis, report writing, and regulatory cross-referencing, the core duties require physical inspections of worksites, manual testing with specialized equipment, and real-time human interaction to conduct safety trainings and emergency investigations.
rows.new_rationale: While these professionals use digital tools for reporting and data analysis, the core of their work is highly dependent on physical site inspections, manual testing, and real-world hazard mitigation. Their role requires significant human interaction for training and enforcement, alongside high-stakes professional judgment regarding safety compliance and incident investigation.

rows.title: Library technicians and assistants
rows.old_rationale: A significant portion of this role involves routine information processing, cataloging, and answering reference questions, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation. While physical tasks like reshelving books and interpersonal community programming provide some protection, the core digital and clerical functions are increasingly being handled by automated systems and AI-driven search tools.
rows.new_rationale: The role involves a significant amount of routine information processing and database management, which are highly susceptible to automation. However, the job also requires physical presence for tasks like shelving and material handling, as well as interpersonal engagement for patron assistance and community programming, which limits the extent to which the role can be fully digitized.

rows.title: Occupational therapists
rows.old_rationale: Occupational therapy is a highly physical and interpersonal profession that requires real-time observation, manual manipulation of patients, and the assessment of physical environments like homes and workplaces. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks such as treatment planning, progress documentation, and medical history analysis, the core of the job involves hands-on rehabilitation and emotional support that cannot be replicated digitally.
rows.new_rationale: Occupational therapy is fundamentally a hands-on, physical profession requiring direct patient interaction, manual manipulation, and real-time observation of physical tasks. While documentation is digital, the core value of the role relies on high-stakes clinical judgment, empathy, and personalized physical intervention that cannot be automated.

rows.title: Physical therapist assistants and aides
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical, hands-on work such as massage, stretching, and assisting patients with mobility, which AI cannot perform. While AI can streamline peripheral tasks like scheduling, documentation, and exercise plan generation, the requirement for real-time human presence and physical dexterity creates a strong barrier to automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is physical, hands-on patient care and manual assistance, which requires constant real-world interaction and physical presence. While there is some routine documentation, the primary value is derived from human empathy, physical dexterity, and the ability to adapt to a patient's immediate physical needs in real-time.

rows.title: Airline and commercial pilots
