rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of high-level digital information processing and critical physical presence. While AI and advanced automation can handle navigation, flight planning, and system monitoring, the role requires real-time human intervention for physical emergencies, complex sensory awareness in unpredictable weather, and the legal/ethical responsibility of passenger safety in the physical world.
rows.new_rationale: While pilots rely heavily on digital cockpit instrumentation and navigation systems, the core of the job is physically operating an aircraft in a high-stakes, real-world environment. The role requires immense professional accountability and split-second human judgment that is difficult to delegate, even as automation handles routine flight path management.

rows.title: Gambling services workers
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical hospitality and digital-adjacent information processing. While AI and automation are significantly impacting sports book writers and slot operations through online platforms and automated betting, the core of the job for dealers and managers involves physical presence, manual dexterity, and real-time human interaction to ensure a 'pleasant experience' and monitor for physical rule violations.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly dependent on physical presence, manual interaction with gaming equipment, and face-to-face customer service. While some information processing occurs, the role is defined by live human interaction and the physical management of the gaming environment, which are difficult to fully automate in a brick-and-mortar setting.

rows.title: Dentists
rows.old_rationale: The core of dentistry involves highly precise physical procedures, manual dexterity, and real-time patient interaction in a clinical setting, which are resistant to AI automation. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic accuracy through X-ray analysis and streamline administrative practice management, the physical requirement of performing surgery and restorative work creates a strong barrier to high exposure.
rows.new_rationale: While dentists increasingly use digital imaging and scanners, the core of the profession is highly manual, requiring physical presence and fine motor dexterity to perform invasive procedures. The role is heavily dependent on high-stakes clinical judgment and patient interaction, both of which are difficult to automate or delegate.

rows.title: Construction and building inspectors
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical site visits and digital knowledge work. AI and computer vision can significantly automate the 'plans examiner' and report-writing functions, while remote inspection technology is already projected to reduce labor demand. However, the core requirement for physical presence to navigate tight spaces, use manual testing equipment, and verify real-world conditions provides a substantial barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is physical site inspection, requiring manual navigation of structures and the use of specialized testing equipment. While there is a digital component to reporting and plan review, the high-stakes nature of safety compliance and the necessity of on-site, real-world verification make this role highly dependent on human judgment and physical presence.

rows.title: Database administrators and architects
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is entirely digital, involving coding (SQL), system architecture, and data management—all areas where AI is exceptionally proficient. AI can already automate routine DBA tasks like performance tuning, query optimization, and security monitoring, while LLMs are highly capable of generating and debugging the complex schemas and scripts used by database architects.
rows.new_rationale: The work is almost entirely digital, involving the design, maintenance, and security of data systems. While much of the technical implementation is routine and highly susceptible to automation, the role requires significant high-stakes judgment regarding data security, architecture strategy, and organizational compliance that is difficult to fully delegate.

rows.title: Butchers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves manual dexterity, physical strength, and real-time interaction with physical products in a cold, unpredictable environment. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like inventory tracking and sales forecasting, the primary duties of cutting, deboning, and handling meat are resistant to digital automation and require specialized robotics rather than AI software.
rows.new_rationale: The role is fundamentally centered on manual labor, physical manipulation of raw materials, and operating in a specialized physical environment. While there is some minor inventory tracking, the core tasks of cutting, deboning, and sanitation are highly physical and rely on human dexterity rather than digital or analytical processing.

rows.title: Librarians and library media specialists
rows.old_rationale: Librarianship is fundamentally about information retrieval, organization, and research—domains where LLMs and AI search engines excel. While the physical presence in schools and public libraries provides a buffer through community programming and interpersonal support, the core technical tasks of cataloging, database management, and reference assistance are highly exposed to AI automation and displacement.
rows.new_rationale: Librarianship is heavily centered on digital information management, research, and database navigation, which are highly susceptible to AI automation. However, the role retains a significant human-centric component involving teaching, community programming, and personalized research assistance that requires empathy and professional judgment.

rows.title: Surgical assistants and technologists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, requiring manual dexterity, sterile technique, and real-time presence in an operating room. While AI and robotics can assist with surgical precision or inventory management, the role remains centered on hands-on patient care and physical equipment handling that cannot be digitized.
rows.new_rationale: The work is almost entirely physical, requiring manual dexterity, sterile environment maintenance, and direct assistance during surgery, which cannot be digitized or automated by current AI. While there is some routine task management (supplies, sterilization), the role requires high-stakes, real-time judgment and close interpersonal coordination within a surgical team where errors have immediate, life-critical consequences.

rows.title: Phlebotomists
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job is a highly tactile, physical procedure (venipuncture) performed on human patients in unpredictable clinical settings, which is resistant to AI automation. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like data entry, sample tracking, and scheduling, the primary value of the role lies in manual dexterity and providing interpersonal emotional support to anxious patients.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is a highly physical, manual task requiring dexterity and direct patient interaction, which is difficult to automate. While there is some digital data entry and labeling, the primary value is in the physical performance of the procedure and the interpersonal management of patient anxiety.

rows.title: Veterinary technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical tasks that AI cannot perform, such as restraining animals, administering anesthesia, performing surgery prep, and providing emergency first aid. However, AI has significant exposure in the diagnostic and administrative aspects of the job, including analyzing lab results, interpreting X-rays, and managing patient records, which may increase individual productivity.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is highly dependent on physical interaction with animals, manual dexterity, and real-world clinical procedures that cannot be digitized. While they perform some routine documentation, the job requires significant human-to-human communication with pet owners and high-stakes judgment regarding animal health, safety, and medical administration.

rows.title: Flight attendants
rows.old_rationale: The core responsibilities of flight attendants are physical and interpersonal, involving safety enforcement, emergency medical response, and manual service tasks that AI cannot perform. While AI may assist with peripheral digital tasks like report writing or passenger manifest management, the role is largely defined by a physical presence required by law for passenger safety.
rows.new_rationale: The role is fundamentally defined by physical presence, manual labor in a cabin environment, and the high-stakes, real-time management of human safety and emergency situations. While some reporting is digital, the core value of the job relies on interpersonal interaction, physical intervention, and professional judgment that cannot be automated or performed remotely.

rows.title: Public relations and fundraising managers
rows.old_rationale: The core digital tasks of this role—writing press releases, drafting speeches, analyzing social media trends, and creating fundraising strategies—are highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. However, the role's heavy reliance on high-stakes interpersonal relationships, such as face-to-face donor meetings and managing media crises, provides a significant human-centric buffer that prevents a higher score.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the drafting and communication work is digital and involves routine information processing, the core of the role relies heavily on high-stakes human relationships, persuasion, and trust-building with donors and the public. The job requires significant professional judgment and accountability, particularly when managing organizational reputation or navigating sensitive legal and ethical communication issues.

rows.title: Insurance underwriters
rows.old_rationale: Underwriting is a fundamentally digital occupation centered on routine information processing, risk modeling, and data analysis—all areas where AI excels. The BLS already projects a decline in employment due to automation, and the shift from traditional software to advanced AI will likely automate even complex, non-standard risk assessments that previously required human judgment.
rows.new_rationale: The role is highly digitized and centered on processing structured data through automated software, making it a prime candidate for AI-driven automation. While complex cases require human judgment and interpersonal communication with agents or medical personnel, the core function is increasingly reliant on algorithmic risk assessment.

rows.title: Sheet metal workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and on-site installation in unpredictable construction environments, which are highly resistant to AI. While AI and computer-controlled machinery (CADD, lasers, and automated presses) are increasingly used in the fabrication phase, the majority of the work—including installation, maintenance, and complex physical maneuvering—cannot be performed by digital tools.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is manual labor, physical installation, and fabrication in real-world environments, which limits the impact of AI. While some fabrication roles utilize CADD and automated machinery, the majority of the work requires physical dexterity, strength, and on-site problem-solving that is highly resistant to digital automation.

rows.title: Electrical and electronics installers and repairers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical manipulation of hardware, manual troubleshooting in unpredictable environments, and the use of hand tools, which are highly resistant to AI automation. While AI can significantly enhance diagnostic software and predictive maintenance schedules, the physical acts of disassembling, repairing, and reassembling complex electrical components require human dexterity and presence.
rows.new_rationale: While these workers use digital diagnostic tools and software, the core of their job is physical, manual repair and installation of hardware in real-world environments. The work requires high-stakes judgment and technical troubleshooting to ensure safety and functionality, but it is fundamentally constrained by the need for physical presence and manual manipulation of equipment.

rows.title: Drywall installers, ceiling tile installers, and tapers
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of this occupation are entirely physical, involving manual labor, dexterity, and movement in unpredictable construction environments. While AI might assist with peripheral administrative tasks or material estimation, it cannot perform the physical installation, cutting, or finishing of drywall and ceiling tiles.
rows.new_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely defined by manual, site-specific physical labor involving the installation and finishing of building materials. It requires minimal digital interaction or routine information processing, as the core tasks are tactile and dependent on physical dexterity and spatial awareness.

rows.title: Veterinary assistants and laboratory animal caretakers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are almost entirely physical and manual, involving the handling, feeding, and restraining of live animals in unpredictable environments. While AI might assist with peripheral administrative tasks or diagnostic data entry, it cannot replace the physical presence and manual dexterity required for animal care, sterilization, and surgical assistance.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is highly physical, involving direct animal handling, cleaning, and medical assistance that cannot be performed digitally. While there is some routine documentation, the job relies primarily on manual dexterity, physical presence, and interpersonal communication with pet owners and veterinary staff.

rows.title: Compensation, benefits, and job analysis specialists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is fundamentally digital and data-driven, involving core tasks like market research, cost analysis, and report generation that align perfectly with AI's strengths. AI can automate the drafting of job descriptions, perform complex compensation benchmarking, and monitor regulatory changes, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the need for manual data processing.
rows.new_rationale: The role is heavily centered on digital data analysis, policy documentation, and administrative processing, making it highly susceptible to automation. While the job requires human interaction for communication and negotiation, the core tasks of benchmarking, classification, and compliance monitoring are increasingly driven by structured data and algorithmic decision support.

rows.title: Advertising sales agents
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves digital knowledge work such as analyzing sales statistics, preparing media kits, and drafting contracts, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation. While the role requires interpersonal relationship building, the shift toward programmatic advertising and AI-driven ad placement is already reducing the need for human intermediaries in the sales process, as reflected in the projected employment decline.
rows.new_rationale: The role is highly digital in its delivery and management of advertising assets, but the core value proposition relies heavily on human persuasion, rapport-building, and negotiation. While routine tasks like drafting contracts and processing paperwork are susceptible to automation, the high-stakes interpersonal nature of sales and the need for customized client solutions remain central to the occupation.

rows.title: Skincare specialists
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job involves physical, hands-on treatments like facials, massages, and hair removal that require real-time human presence and manual dexterity. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like skin analysis, product recommendations, and business management, it cannot replace the tactile and interpersonal nature of the primary service.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, requiring direct, hands-on manipulation of a client's skin and body in a specific, sanitary environment. While there is some information processing involved in skin analysis and business management, the primary value is derived from interpersonal trust, empathy, and the physical application of professional services that cannot be automated.

rows.title: Electrical and electronic engineering technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of physical labor, such as assembling prototypes, soldering circuitry, and repairing hardware, which provides a buffer against full automation. However, a substantial portion of the role involves digital tasks like drafting schematics, analyzing test data, and writing technical reports—all areas where AI is becoming highly proficient. AI will likely augment their troubleshooting capabilities and automate the documentation aspects of the job, increasing individual productivity but potentially reducing overall demand.
rows.new_rationale: This role involves significant digital work through CAD, reporting, and data analysis, but it is fundamentally anchored in the physical world through the assembly, calibration, and repair of hardware. While they perform routine testing and documentation, the job requires hands-on manual dexterity and physical presence in labs or factories, which limits the extent to which AI can fully automate the core tasks.

rows.title: Rehabilitation counselors
rows.old_rationale: Rehabilitation counseling involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, such as developing rehabilitation plans, maintaining records, and researching resources, which are highly susceptible to AI assistance. However, the core of the profession relies on high-stakes interpersonal interaction, empathy, and physical advocacy for clients with disabilities, creating a natural barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is deeply rooted in human interaction, empathy, and high-stakes clinical judgment regarding a client's life and career path, which are difficult to automate. While documentation and resource lookup are digital and routine, the primary value is derived from the interpersonal relationship and the professional accountability required to manage complex, individualized rehabilitation plans.

rows.title: Environmental scientists and specialists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, including data analysis, report writing, and regulatory compliance, which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the role is anchored by a physical component involving fieldwork, site inspections, and laboratory sample analysis that AI cannot currently replicate. AI will likely serve as a powerful tool for modeling environmental impacts and drafting technical documents, increasing individual productivity while leaving the physical data collection and stakeholder relationship management to humans.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the work involves digital data analysis and reporting, the role requires significant physical interaction with environmental sites and samples. The high level of judgment and accountability stems from the need to interpret complex scientific data to ensure regulatory compliance and public health safety, which are difficult to fully automate.

rows.title: Diagnostic medical sonographers
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical and digital work; while AI is rapidly advancing in image analysis and diagnostic assistance, the role requires significant physical presence to position patients and manipulate transducers with high hand-eye coordination. Furthermore, the interpersonal aspect of patient care and the real-time physical adjustments needed during a scan provide a substantial barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the work involves digital imaging technology, the core of the role is highly dependent on physical interaction with patients and the manual manipulation of ultrasound transducers to capture specific anatomical views. The job requires significant human empathy and communication skills, alongside high-stakes professional judgment in identifying abnormalities that directly influence medical diagnoses.

rows.title: Ironworkers
rows.old_rationale: Ironworking is a highly physical occupation performed in unpredictable, outdoor environments and at great heights, which provides a significant natural barrier to AI and robotics. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like blueprint analysis or site scheduling, the core duties of manual welding, bolting, and balancing on structural beams require real-time human dexterity and physical presence that cannot be automated with current or near-future technology.
rows.new_rationale: Ironworking is a highly manual, site-specific trade that relies on physical strength, balance, and real-world manipulation of heavy materials. While workers must interpret blueprints and exercise safety-critical judgment, the core of the job is physical labor that is largely insulated from digital automation.

rows.title: Film and video editors and camera operators
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical camera operation and digital video editing. While camera operators have a physical barrier to AI due to the need for on-site equipment management and real-world positioning, editors work entirely in digital environments where AI is rapidly automating tasks like color grading, sound syncing, and even rough-cut generation. The rise of generative video and AI-driven post-production tools significantly increases the productivity of each worker, potentially reducing the total number of editors needed for complex projects.
rows.new_rationale: The work is highly digital, as editing is almost entirely computer-based and camera operation increasingly involves digital interfaces and robotics. However, the role retains significant physical requirements for on-location filming and high-stakes creative judgment in collaborating with directors to realize a specific artistic vision.

rows.title: Small engine mechanics
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical manipulation of mechanical components, manual dexterity, and real-world troubleshooting in unpredictable environments. While AI can assist with diagnostic software and technical documentation, it cannot perform the physical labor of disassembling engines, replacing parts, or conducting hands-on repairs.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is manual, physical labor involving the disassembly and repair of mechanical equipment in real-world environments. While some diagnostic tools are computerized, the work is fundamentally defined by physical dexterity and site-specific interaction with machinery rather than digital information processing.

rows.title: Property appraisers and assessors
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves analyzing data, comparing market trends, and generating reports, all of which are highly susceptible to Automated Valuation Models (AVMs) and AI-driven data analysis. While physical site inspections and the need to defend assessments in public hearings provide a buffer, the shift toward 'desktop appraisals' and AI-assisted mass assessments significantly reduces the human labor required per valuation.
rows.new_rationale: The role relies heavily on digital databases, software, and automated valuation models for data analysis and reporting. While site visits require physical presence to inspect property conditions, the core analytical work is increasingly digitized, though it remains subject to professional accountability and the need to defend valuations in public or legal settings.

rows.title: Health education specialists
rows.old_rationale: A significant portion of the role involves digital knowledge work such as analyzing community health data, writing grant proposals, and creating educational materials, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the core requirements of community advocacy, empathetic interpersonal interaction, and physical presence for local events and training provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While health education specialists use digital tools for data analysis and material creation, the core of their work relies heavily on empathy, interpersonal communication, and building trust within diverse communities. The role requires significant professional judgment to tailor health interventions to specific populations, which limits the extent to which these high-stakes, human-centric interactions can be automated.

rows.title: Aerospace engineers
rows.old_rationale: Aerospace engineering is a high-level knowledge occupation where core tasks like aerodynamic modeling, structural analysis, and propulsion design are increasingly performed using digital simulation and AI-driven optimization tools. While the role requires critical human judgment for safety, regulatory compliance, and physical inspection of prototypes, the heavy reliance on computer-aided design (CAD) and complex data analysis makes it highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and task automation.
rows.new_rationale: Aerospace engineering relies heavily on digital design, simulation, and modeling tools, though the work is fundamentally tethered to physical safety, testing, and real-world performance. The role requires high-stakes professional judgment and accountability for complex systems where errors can have catastrophic consequences, making it difficult to fully automate despite the high degree of digital information processing.

rows.title: Medical equipment repairers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical and manual, requiring dexterity to handle tools, disassemble machinery, and work in tight physical spaces. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic software, troubleshooting, and predictive maintenance scheduling, the physical act of repairing, installing, and calibrating hardware remains a human-centric task that cannot be automated by digital AI alone.
rows.new_rationale: While the role involves significant digital interaction through software calibration and network integration, the core of the job is physical, hands-on repair of complex medical hardware. The work carries high accountability due to the life-critical nature of the equipment, but the physical requirement for site-specific, manual intervention makes it highly resistant to full automation.

rows.title: Insulation workers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are highly physical and require manual dexterity in unpredictable, confined environments like attics and crawl spaces. AI has minimal impact on the physical installation of materials, though it may slightly assist with peripheral tasks like blueprint analysis or material estimation.
rows.new_rationale: The work of an insulation worker is almost entirely manual and physical, requiring site-specific labor, dexterity, and the use of physical tools in varied environments. While there is a minor component of reading blueprints and calculating measurements, the core tasks are not digital or information-based, making the role highly resistant to AI automation.

rows.title: Community health workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is built on trust, cultural sensitivity, and physical presence within specific communities, which AI cannot replicate. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like data collection, report writing, and resource matching, the primary duties involve home visits, hands-on advocacy, and real-time interpersonal coaching for vulnerable populations.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is built on high-touch, in-person human interaction, empathy, and physical presence within communities, which are difficult to automate. While some recordkeeping is digital, the primary value is derived from trust-based relationships, cultural sensitivity, and navigating complex real-world social and health environments.

rows.title: Financial examiners
rows.old_rationale: Financial examiners perform work that is almost entirely digital, involving the analysis of balance sheets, loan documentation, and regulatory text. AI is exceptionally well-suited for automating the core tasks of this role, such as identifying anomalies in financial data, summarizing meeting minutes, and checking compliance against complex legal frameworks, though human judgment remains necessary for final risk assessments and high-level management evaluations.
rows.new_rationale: The core of the work involves analyzing digital financial records and documentation, which is highly susceptible to AI-driven automation. However, the role requires significant professional judgment, accountability for regulatory compliance, and interpersonal coordination with bank management, which limits the extent to which the entire job can be delegated to machines.

rows.title: Glaziers
rows.old_rationale: The core work of a glazier is highly physical, requiring manual dexterity, strength, and real-time problem-solving in unpredictable construction environments. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like blueprint analysis or precision measurements, the actual installation, cutting, and securing of glass in the physical world remain well beyond current AI and robotic capabilities.
rows.new_rationale: The work of a glazier is fundamentally manual and site-specific, requiring physical strength, dexterity, and the ability to operate in complex, real-world construction environments. While they must interpret blueprints, the core of the job involves physical installation and material handling that is highly resistant to automation.

rows.title: Surveying and mapping technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical fieldwork and digital data processing. While AI and automation (via drones and GIS software) significantly enhance data analysis, map creation, and calculation tasks, the role still requires a physical presence to navigate rugged terrain, set markers, and operate equipment in the real world.
rows.new_rationale: The role is highly split between significant physical fieldwork in rugged terrain and digital office work involving GIS, CAD, and data processing. While the data processing and mapping components are increasingly automated, the job requires physical presence for data collection and human judgment to ensure the accuracy of the automated outputs.

rows.title: Tax examiners and collectors, and revenue agents
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is highly exposed because its core tasks—reviewing financial documents, identifying discrepancies, and interpreting tax law—are digital information-processing tasks where AI excels. While revenue agents handling complex corporate audits and collectors performing field investigations require human judgment and interpersonal skills, a significant portion of the routine examination and data verification work is already being automated or enhanced by sophisticated algorithms.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role involves processing digital financial data, tax returns, and records, making it highly susceptible to automation in information processing. While some roles require field audits or face-to-face interaction, the majority of the work is administrative and analytical, though it retains a significant requirement for professional judgment and accountability in legal and financial enforcement.

rows.title: Chiropractors
rows.old_rationale: The core of chiropractic work is highly physical, requiring manual dexterity for spinal adjustments and real-time physical assessment of a patient's musculoskeletal system. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like medical imaging analysis, diagnostic suggestions, and administrative business management, it cannot replace the essential hands-on therapeutic interventions that define the profession.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical, hands-on manipulation of the human body, which cannot be automated or digitized. While digital tools are used for record-keeping and diagnostic imaging, the primary value is derived from high-stakes clinical judgment, physical dexterity, and the empathetic, trust-based relationship between the practitioner and the patient.

rows.title: Tour and travel guides
rows.old_rationale: While AI can automate the digital aspects of the job—such as researching history, planning itineraries, and providing multilingual translations—the core of the role requires physical presence and real-time human interaction. Guides must physically escort groups through unpredictable environments, manage group dynamics, and provide a personal, empathetic experience that digital tools cannot fully replicate.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is physical presence, real-time interaction, and group management in specific locations, which are highly resistant to AI automation. While some administrative tasks like booking or research involve digital tools, the primary value proposition is the human-led, experiential, and social nature of the service.

rows.title: Occupational therapy assistants and aides
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical assistance, manual therapy, and real-time human interaction, such as lifting patients and guiding motor skill exercises, which AI cannot perform. While AI can streamline administrative tasks like scheduling, documentation, and billing, the primary value of the role is rooted in physical presence and empathetic interpersonal support.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is physical, hands-on patient care and manual equipment management, which requires constant physical presence and interaction. While there is some administrative documentation, the primary value is derived from human empathy, physical assistance, and the high-stakes judgment required to safely guide patients through therapeutic activities.

rows.title: Lodging managers
rows.old_rationale: Lodging managers perform a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical site management. While AI can automate back-office tasks like revenue management, dynamic pricing, and staff scheduling, the core role requires physical inspections of facilities and high-stakes interpersonal conflict resolution with guests and staff that cannot be fully digitized.
rows.new_rationale: While lodging managers rely on digital systems for reservations and financial tracking, the core of the role is deeply embedded in the physical environment of the facility and the management of human interactions. The job requires high-stakes, real-time judgment to resolve guest complaints and manage staff, which necessitates a physical presence and interpersonal skills that are difficult to automate.

rows.title: Hazardous materials removal workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor in unpredictable environments, such as scrubbing surfaces, constructing containment areas, and operating heavy machinery. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like record-keeping, regulatory compliance checks, or analyzing sensor data for radiation levels, the primary work requires a physical human presence to navigate complex, hazardous sites.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, involving manual labor, the operation of heavy machinery, and direct interaction with hazardous environments that require human presence. While there is some record-keeping and adherence to safety protocols, the work is fundamentally defined by physical manipulation of materials rather than digital information processing.

rows.title: Budget analysts
rows.old_rationale: Budget analysts perform work that is almost entirely digital, involving data analysis, financial modeling, and report writing—all areas where AI is highly capable. While the role requires human negotiation and communication with stakeholders, the core tasks of consolidating budgets, identifying discrepancies, and performing cost-benefit analyses are highly susceptible to automation and significant productivity gains through AI.
rows.new_rationale: The role is highly digital and centered on routine information processing, such as analyzing financial data and drafting budget reports. While the work requires significant human interaction for communication and persuasion, and carries accountability for financial recommendations, it has almost no physical world dependency.

rows.title: News analysts, reporters, and journalists
rows.old_rationale: The core work product is digital (text, scripts, and multimedia), and AI can already automate significant portions of research, drafting, and editing. While physical presence for field reporting and the cultivation of human sources provide a protective barrier, the overall occupation faces high exposure as AI tools drastically increase individual productivity, potentially leading to further industry consolidation and fewer required roles.
rows.new_rationale: The work is highly digital in its creation and distribution, and involves significant routine processing of information and drafting. However, it remains heavily dependent on human interaction for interviews and relationship-building, as well as high-stakes editorial judgment and accountability that are difficult to fully automate.

rows.title: Training and development managers
rows.old_rationale: This role is predominantly digital and knowledge-based, involving the creation of instructional content, data-driven needs assessments, and budget management—all areas where AI is highly capable. While the role requires significant human-centric leadership and stakeholder collaboration, AI can automate the generation of training materials, personalize learning paths, and analyze program effectiveness, substantially increasing the productivity of each manager.
rows.new_rationale: The role is highly digital in its delivery and management of training content, yet it remains deeply rooted in human-centric activities like leadership, negotiation with stakeholders, and organizational culture building. While information processing is a significant component, the core value lies in high-stakes judgment regarding organizational strategy and the nuanced interpersonal management of staff and trainees.

rows.title: Concierges
rows.old_rationale: The role is a hybrid of digital information processing and physical hospitality. While AI can automate core tasks like making reservations, providing local recommendations, and answering emails, the job still requires a physical presence for greeting guests, handling packages, and providing high-touch interpersonal service that is central to the luxury and residential experience.
rows.new_rationale: While concierges use digital tools for bookings and communication, the core of the role is rooted in physical presence and high-touch interpersonal service. The job requires significant human interaction and empathy to manage guest experiences, though many of the routine information-processing tasks are increasingly susceptible to automation.

rows.title: Urban and regional planners
rows.old_rationale: Urban planners perform heavy knowledge work including data analysis, GIS mapping, and report writing, all of which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement and automation. However, the role requires significant physical site inspections and high-stakes interpersonal negotiation with government officials and the public, which provides a buffer against total automation.
rows.new_rationale: Urban planners rely heavily on digital tools like GIS and data visualization for analysis, but their work is fundamentally anchored in high-stakes human negotiation, public policy, and site-specific physical context. While data processing is a core task, the final recommendations require complex professional judgment and accountability that are difficult to fully automate.

rows.title: Logging workers
rows.old_rationale: The core work is highly physical, involving the operation of heavy machinery and manual labor in unpredictable outdoor environments. While AI may improve peripheral tasks like log grading through computer vision or optimize harvest planning, the physical requirements of felling and transporting timber provide a strong barrier to AI replacement.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is manual labor and the operation of heavy machinery in unpredictable, outdoor environments, which is highly resistant to digital automation. While some roles involve basic data entry for grading, the vast majority of the work requires physical presence, real-time spatial awareness, and manual manipulation of the physical world.

rows.title: Medical transcriptionists
rows.old_rationale: Medical transcription is a purely digital, routine information-processing task that has already been heavily impacted by speech recognition. Modern AI and Large Language Models (LLMs) can now handle complex medical terminology, formatting, and summarization with high accuracy, transforming the human role into a diminishing oversight function or eliminating it entirely.
rows.new_rationale: The work is almost entirely digital, consisting of processing and editing text generated from voice recordings within electronic health record systems. While the role requires accuracy to ensure patient safety, the core tasks are highly routine and increasingly automated by speech recognition software, leading to a significant decline in the occupation's outlook.

rows.title: Environmental science and protection technicians
rows.old_rationale: The core of this role involves physical fieldwork, such as collecting soil and water samples and inspecting facilities, which AI cannot perform. However, AI will significantly impact the digital aspects of the job, including data analysis, the preparation of reports and charts, and the modeling of environmental impacts.
rows.new_rationale: The role is heavily dependent on physical fieldwork, sample collection, and site inspections, which limits the potential for full AI automation. While data processing and reporting are digital and routine, the core value of the job lies in the physical interaction with the environment and the accountability involved in regulatory compliance and public health safety.

rows.title: Archivists, curators, and museum workers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical preservation. AI will significantly automate archival tasks like cataloging, metadata generation, and electronic record management, while also assisting curators with research and exhibit design. However, the physical requirements of handling artifacts, restoring delicate items, and managing in-person public interactions provide a substantial buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While these roles increasingly involve digital cataloging and electronic record management, the core of the work remains deeply rooted in the physical handling, preservation, and exhibition of tangible artifacts. High-stakes judgment is required for the authentication, appraisal, and ethical stewardship of historical items, which cannot be easily delegated to automated systems.

rows.title: Announcers and DJs
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is split between highly exposed digital work and less exposed physical work. Broadcast and radio roles are under significant threat from AI-generated voices and automated content curation, whereas live event DJs and emcees retain a physical and interpersonal barrier that is harder to automate.
rows.new_rationale: Broadcast announcers and DJs rely heavily on digital production software and media delivery, making them highly susceptible to automation in content generation and scheduling. While live event DJs require physical presence and human interaction, the core broadcast role is increasingly defined by routine information processing and digital content delivery that AI can replicate.

rows.title: Environmental engineers
rows.old_rationale: Environmental engineering involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, including data analysis, system design, and report writing, which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the role is insulated by the necessity for physical site inspections, regulatory compliance monitoring in the field, and the legal accountability associated with Professional Engineer (PE) licensure.
rows.new_rationale: Environmental engineers rely heavily on digital tools for modeling, reporting, and data analysis, but their work is fundamentally anchored in physical site inspections, remediation, and real-world system design. The role requires high-stakes professional judgment and accountability, particularly regarding legal compliance and public safety, which limits the extent to which AI can autonomously perform the core responsibilities.

rows.title: Agricultural and food science technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of physical labor, such as collecting biological samples, maintaining farm equipment, and operating laboratory hardware, which provides a buffer against full automation. However, AI will heavily impact the data-heavy portions of the job, including compiling test results, analyzing chemical properties, and generating reports, making technicians more productive but potentially reducing the number of workers needed for data processing.
rows.new_rationale: The role is heavily dependent on physical interaction with samples, laboratory equipment, and agricultural environments, which limits the scope for AI automation. While data recording and analysis are routine and digital, the core of the job requires manual manipulation and on-site observation that cannot be fully replaced by digital systems.

rows.title: Agricultural and food scientists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, including data analysis, research synthesis, and report writing, which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement. However, the role is anchored by physical requirements such as field sampling, laboratory experiments, and on-site inspections at farms or processing plants that AI cannot currently replicate.
rows.new_rationale: While these scientists use digital tools for data analysis and reporting, their work is fundamentally anchored in physical-world experimentation, field sampling, and site-specific inspections. The role requires high-stakes professional judgment regarding food safety, environmental impact, and biological research that cannot be fully automated or delegated to AI.

rows.title: Mechanical engineering technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical labor. While AI can significantly automate digital tasks like 3D modeling, data analysis, and cost estimation, the role still requires a substantial physical presence for assembling parts, setting up vehicle crash tests, and operating machinery like lathes and grinders.
rows.new_rationale: The role involves significant digital work through 3D design software and data analysis, but it is equally defined by physical, hands-on tasks like assembling parts, operating machinery, and conducting real-world tests. While they perform routine documentation and testing, their work requires physical presence and manual skill, making it a hybrid role that is partially susceptible to automation but constrained by the need for physical interaction with hardware.

rows.title: Jewelers and precious stone and metal workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job involves physical dexterity, manual repair, and hands-on craftsmanship that AI cannot replicate. However, AI and automation are significantly impacting the design and appraisal phases through generative CAD tools and automated gemstone grading, contributing to a projected decline in total employment.
rows.new_rationale: While the occupation increasingly utilizes CAD/CAM and 3D printing, the core of the work remains highly dependent on manual dexterity, physical manipulation of materials, and site-specific bench work. The role requires significant professional judgment in appraising and grading, but the physical nature of the craftsmanship and the necessity of direct human interaction for sales and custom design limit the extent to which AI can fully automate the profession.

rows.title: Mathematicians and statisticians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital, involving data analysis, mathematical modeling, and coding—all domains where AI and Large Language Models excel. While high-level theoretical research and complex interdisciplinary communication require human judgment, AI can now automate significant portions of data cleaning, statistical testing, and code generation, drastically increasing individual productivity and restructuring the workflow.
rows.new_rationale: The work is highly digital and involves significant routine information processing through statistical software and modeling. However, the role requires high-level judgment to interpret complex data, design experiments, and communicate findings to stakeholders, which limits the extent to which the core analytical responsibility can be fully delegated to AI.

rows.title: Athletic trainers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of athletic trainers—such as applying tape/braces, providing emergency first aid on the field, and performing manual rehabilitation—require physical presence and tactile skills that AI cannot replicate. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like injury analysis, record-keeping, and designing recovery protocols, the job remains fundamentally grounded in real-time human interaction and physical healthcare delivery.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is physical, hands-on medical intervention and emergency care that requires real-time presence and manual manipulation. While administrative tasks involve digital record-keeping, the primary value is derived from high-stakes clinical judgment and the interpersonal trust required to treat patients in pain.

rows.title: Actuaries
rows.old_rationale: Actuarial work is fundamentally digital, involving the analysis of large datasets, statistical modeling, and financial forecasting—all areas where AI and machine learning excel. While the profession requires high-level human judgment and the ability to explain complex risks to stakeholders, the core technical tasks of data compilation and probability estimation are highly susceptible to automation and significant productivity gains through AI.
rows.new_rationale: Actuarial work is highly digital and involves significant routine information processing through statistical modeling and database analysis. However, the role requires high-stakes professional judgment, regulatory accountability, and the ability to communicate complex findings to stakeholders, which limits the extent to which the entire job can be automated.

rows.title: Stationary engineers and boiler operators
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical maintenance, manual repairs, and hands-on troubleshooting of heavy mechanical equipment in unpredictable physical environments. While AI and automated control systems can enhance monitoring and data logging, they cannot perform the physical tasks of overhauling valves, lubricating parts, or crawling inside boilers for inspections.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is physical interaction with heavy machinery, requiring manual dexterity, site-specific maintenance, and physical presence to handle repairs and safety inspections. While they monitor digital gauges and computerized controls, the work is fundamentally grounded in the physical world, and the high-stakes nature of operating industrial boilers necessitates significant professional accountability for safety and operational integrity.

rows.title: Semiconductor processing technicians
rows.old_rationale: The role involves a significant physical component, requiring presence in cleanrooms to handle wafers, maintain equipment, and perform manual inspections. However, AI and advanced automation are increasingly capable of monitoring process data, optimizing machine parameters, and performing high-precision quality control, which automates the information-processing and monitoring aspects of the job.
rows.new_rationale: The role is heavily dependent on physical presence in a sterile cleanroom environment to operate and maintain specialized manufacturing machinery. While the work involves digital interfaces and data logging, the core tasks are manual and site-specific, requiring high levels of dexterity and physical oversight of automated processes.

rows.title: Solar photovoltaic installers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, involving manual labor, climbing roofs, and using hand tools in unpredictable outdoor environments. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like system design optimization or site measurements via computer vision, the actual installation and maintenance require human dexterity and physical presence that current and near-future AI/robotics cannot replicate.
rows.new_rationale: The work of a solar photovoltaic installer is overwhelmingly physical, requiring manual labor, tool operation, and site-specific construction at heights. While there is some reliance on technical documentation and basic math, the core tasks are manual and cannot be performed digitally or through automated information processing.

rows.title: Physicists and astronomers
rows.old_rationale: Physicists and astronomers perform high-level knowledge work that is increasingly digital, including data analysis, mathematical modeling, and software development. While AI significantly accelerates data processing and literature review, the core requirements of original theoretical innovation, experimental design, and complex physical-world problem solving provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the work involves high-level digital data analysis and modeling, the role is heavily anchored in physical experimentation, equipment design, and the interpretation of natural phenomena. The high-stakes nature of scientific discovery, research funding, and professional accountability for experimental outcomes makes this role difficult to fully automate despite the high degree of digital integration.

rows.title: Air traffic controllers
rows.old_rationale: The core work is fundamentally digital and data-driven, involving the monitoring of radar screens and the processing of complex spatial information to ensure aircraft separation. While the high-stakes nature of safety and the need for real-time human communication with pilots provide a buffer, AI and automated systems (like NextGen) are already increasing productivity and handling more traffic per controller, leading to a very low projected growth in human employment.
rows.new_rationale: The role is highly digital and involves constant processing of structured information (radar, flight plans, weather data) to manage traffic flow. However, it remains deeply dependent on physical-world outcomes and high-stakes, real-time human judgment, where the accountability for safety makes full automation extremely difficult to implement.

rows.title: Exercise physiologists
rows.old_rationale: The role involves a significant amount of physical presence, including conducting stress tests, measuring vital signs with medical equipment, and providing hands-on rehabilitative care. While AI can highly automate the digital aspects of the job—such as analyzing medical history, processing biometric data, and generating personalized exercise regimens—the core requirement for real-time human interaction, physical assessment, and clinical supervision in a medical setting limits its total exposure.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role involves direct, hands-on physical interaction with patients to monitor health indicators and guide exercise, making it highly dependent on the physical world and interpersonal engagement. While they use digital tools for documentation and data analysis, the high-stakes nature of managing patients with chronic conditions requires professional judgment and accountability that cannot be automated.

rows.title: Fishing and hunting workers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties are highly physical and take place in unpredictable outdoor environments, requiring manual labor, mechanical repair, and real-time physical coordination. AI's impact is limited to peripheral enhancements in fish-finding sonar, GPS navigation, and regulatory data processing, but it cannot replace the physical act of hauling nets, trapping animals, or maintaining vessels.
rows.new_rationale: The work is overwhelmingly physical, involving manual labor, navigation of unpredictable environments, and direct interaction with wildlife. While digital tools like GPS and sonar are used for navigation and locating catch, the core tasks of harvesting, sorting, and vessel maintenance are manual and cannot be automated by AI. The role requires situational judgment regarding safety and weather, but this is highly localized and dependent on real-time physical conditions rather than information processing.

rows.title: Chemical engineers
rows.old_rationale: Chemical engineering involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, including process simulation, CAD design, and data analysis, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven optimization. However, the role is anchored by the need for physical presence in laboratories and industrial plants to monitor equipment, ensure safety compliance, and troubleshoot hardware in the real world.
rows.new_rationale: Chemical engineering relies heavily on digital tools like CAD and simulation software, but the core work is deeply tethered to physical manufacturing plants, laboratories, and real-world safety compliance. While they perform analytical tasks, the high-stakes nature of chemical safety and process design requires significant professional judgment and accountability that cannot be easily automated.

rows.title: Microbiologists
rows.old_rationale: Microbiologists have a high exposure to AI because a significant portion of their work involves data analysis, pattern recognition in genetic sequences, and technical reporting—all areas where AI is rapidly advancing. However, the physical requirement to conduct laboratory experiments, maintain biological cultures, and collect field samples provides a substantial buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While microbiologists use digital tools for data analysis and reporting, the core of their work is highly dependent on physical laboratory experimentation, sample handling, and real-world observation. The role requires significant high-stakes professional judgment in interpreting scientific results and ensuring safety, which is difficult to fully automate or delegate to AI.

rows.title: Petroleum engineers
rows.old_rationale: Petroleum engineering involves significant digital knowledge work, such as reservoir modeling, data analysis, and drilling simulation, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven optimization and automation. However, the role remains anchored by the necessity of physical site visits to remote or offshore locations to oversee equipment installation and troubleshoot real-world mechanical issues that AI cannot physically address.
rows.new_rationale: While petroleum engineers rely heavily on digital modeling and data analysis, their work is fundamentally tied to physical, high-stakes environments like drilling rigs and underground reservoirs. The role requires significant professional accountability for safety and operational success, which limits the extent to which AI can autonomously manage the complex, site-specific physical challenges of the job.

rows.title: Athletes and sports competitors
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical performance, manual dexterity, and real-time human presence in the physical world, which AI cannot replicate. While AI is used as a peripheral tool for performance analytics, strategy optimization, and diet planning, it does not replace the athlete's physical labor or the entertainment value of human competition.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical performance, training, and competition, which are inherently tied to the physical world and cannot be digitized or automated. While athletes use video analysis and strategy, these are secondary to the primary requirement of physical presence and athletic skill, making the role highly resistant to AI displacement.

rows.title: Zoologists and wildlife biologists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical fieldwork and digital knowledge work. AI significantly impacts the data-heavy aspects of the job, such as analyzing GIS data, processing camera trap imagery, and modeling population dynamics, but it cannot replace the physical requirements of collecting biological specimens, handling wild animals, or navigating remote outdoor environments.
rows.new_rationale: While these professionals increasingly use digital modeling and GIS tools for data analysis, the core of their work is deeply rooted in physical, site-specific fieldwork and the direct handling of biological specimens. Their role requires high-stakes scientific judgment and accountability for conservation outcomes, which are difficult to automate or delegate to AI systems.

rows.title: Court reporters and simultaneous captioners
rows.old_rationale: The core product of this occupation is a verbatim digital transcript, a task where AI speech-to-text and natural language processing are already highly proficient. While legal requirements for human oversight and the need to clarify inaudible speech provide a temporary buffer, the rapid advancement of AI in transcription and real-time captioning creates extreme pressure for automation and massive productivity gains that reduce the need for human workers.
rows.new_rationale: The core function of this role is the conversion of speech to digital text, which is highly susceptible to automation via advanced speech-to-text and AI technologies. While the work requires high accuracy and accountability for legal records, the primary tasks of transcription and documentation are inherently routine and digital in nature.

rows.title: Fire inspectors
rows.old_rationale: The role requires a significant physical presence for onsite inspections, evidence collection in debris, and forest patrolling, which provides a natural barrier to AI. However, AI can substantially assist with digital tasks such as reviewing building blueprints, analyzing fire scene data, and automating the documentation of code violations and legal reports.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is physical site inspection, evidence collection, and hazard assessment in real-world environments, which are difficult to automate. While documentation and code lookup are digital, the job relies heavily on human judgment, legal accountability, and interpersonal interaction with property owners and witnesses.

rows.title: Recreational therapists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical presence, manual assistance, and real-time interpersonal interaction through activities like adaptive sports, dance, and community outings. While AI can significantly assist with digital tasks like patient assessment, treatment planning, and progress documentation, it cannot replace the physical and emotional labor required to lead therapeutic interventions in person.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is highly dependent on physical presence, direct human interaction, and empathy, which are difficult to automate. While documentation and planning involve digital tools, the therapeutic interventions themselves require real-world, site-specific engagement and high-stakes clinical judgment regarding patient well-being.

rows.title: Audiologists
rows.old_rationale: Audiology is a clinical healthcare profession that requires physical presence for examinations, wax removal, and fitting hardware like hearing aids or cochlear implants. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic accuracy, signal processing in hearing devices, and automated testing, the core of the job involves interpersonal counseling, physical patient interaction, and complex clinical judgment that cannot be fully digitized.
rows.new_rationale: While audiologists use digital diagnostic tools and record-keeping, the core of the profession relies on physical, in-person clinical examinations, manual procedures, and the fitting of devices. The role is highly dependent on complex human interaction, empathy, and high-stakes clinical judgment that requires professional licensure and accountability.

rows.title: Wind turbine technicians
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves high-stakes physical labor, including climbing 200-foot towers, rappelling, and performing manual repairs in confined spaces. While AI can assist with remote monitoring and predictive maintenance diagnostics, the physical requirement to replace components and perform manual inspections in unpredictable outdoor environments provides a strong barrier to automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is highly physical, requiring manual labor, climbing, and onsite mechanical repair in extreme environments that cannot be automated. While digital tools are used for diagnostics and data logging, the physical nature of the work and the high-stakes safety requirements for maintenance make it largely resistant to AI displacement.

rows.title: Epidemiologists
rows.old_rationale: Epidemiology is a data-intensive knowledge profession where core tasks like statistical analysis, pattern recognition in large datasets, and literature reviews are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. While the role requires human judgment for policy-making, community outreach, and field investigations, the shift toward digital health records and automated surveillance systems significantly increases the productivity and exposure of the occupation.
rows.new_rationale: Epidemiologists rely heavily on digital data analysis, statistical modeling, and documentation, which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the role requires significant human-centric tasks like community outreach, policy negotiation, and high-stakes professional judgment regarding public health interventions that are difficult to fully automate.

rows.title: Forest and conservation workers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are highly physical and performed in unpredictable outdoor environments, such as planting seedlings, clearing brush, and suppressing forest fires. While AI and remote sensing can assist with peripheral tasks like tree counting and health monitoring, the primary labor-intensive functions cannot be automated by digital AI systems.
rows.new_rationale: The work is almost entirely manual and physical, taking place in remote, outdoor environments that require significant physical stamina and direct interaction with the natural world. While some routine tasks like tree counting are being augmented by remote sensing technology, the core of the job remains labor-intensive, site-specific, and low in digital or administrative complexity.

rows.title: Podiatrists
rows.old_rationale: Podiatry is a medical specialty that requires significant physical presence for exams, manual procedures, and complex surgeries that AI cannot perform. However, AI will heavily impact the knowledge-based aspects of the role, such as analyzing X-rays, diagnosing conditions from medical history, and automating clinical documentation and administrative tasks.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation involves high-stakes physical interventions, such as surgery and manual examinations, which are inherently resistant to automation. While digital tools are used for diagnostics and records, the reliance on human empathy, professional accountability for patient health, and complex physical manipulation makes this role highly protected from AI displacement.

rows.title: Atmospheric scientists, including meteorologists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves analyzing digital data, running computer models, and writing code, all of which are highly susceptible to AI advancement. Machine learning is already revolutionizing weather forecasting by outperforming traditional numerical models in speed and efficiency, potentially reducing the number of human forecasters needed for routine data processing and report generation.
rows.new_rationale: The core work relies heavily on digital computer modeling, data analysis, and software-based visualization. While the subject matter is the physical atmosphere, the actual work is largely performed through digital interfaces and mathematical simulations, though high-stakes judgment is required for issuing life-saving weather warnings.

rows.title: Anthropologists and archeologists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical fieldwork and digital knowledge work. While AI can significantly automate data analysis, pattern recognition in artifacts, and report writing, it cannot replace the physical requirements of excavation, site surveying, and in-person ethnographic observation.
rows.new_rationale: While these professionals use digital tools for analysis and reporting, their core work is heavily anchored in physical fieldwork, excavation, and direct observation of human behavior or artifacts. The role requires significant high-stakes judgment in interpreting complex cultural or historical data and navigating interpersonal relationships during research, which limits the potential for full automation.

rows.title: Political scientists
rows.old_rationale: Political science is a purely digital knowledge-work occupation centered on data analysis, research, and writing, all of which are core strengths of generative AI and LLMs. While high-level strategic judgment and interpersonal networking remain human-centric, AI can now automate the collection of public opinion data, the drafting of policy briefs, and the forecasting of political trends, leading to significant productivity gains and a projected decline in total employment.
rows.new_rationale: The work is heavily centered on digital research, data analysis, and report writing, making it highly compatible with digital tools. While the role involves significant information processing, it also requires high-level judgment to interpret complex political trends and accountability for the policy recommendations provided, which limits the extent to which AI can fully replace the professional's role.

rows.title: Hydrologists
rows.old_rationale: Hydrologists have a high exposure to AI because a significant portion of their work involves data analysis, computer modeling, and report writing—tasks where AI is rapidly advancing. However, the physical requirement of field work, such as collecting water samples and measuring streamflow in remote locations, provides a substantial buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: Hydrologists rely heavily on digital modeling and data analysis, but their work is fundamentally anchored in physical field data collection and site-specific environmental assessments. The role requires high-stakes professional judgment for public safety and resource management, which limits the extent to which the core decision-making can be fully automated.

rows.title: Desktop publishers
rows.old_rationale: The work product is entirely digital and involves routine information processing, layout design, and basic editing—all areas where AI is highly proficient. Generative AI and automated layout tools can now handle text integration, image adjustment, and formatting with minimal human intervention, leading to a projected decline in the occupation as these tasks are automated or absorbed by other roles using AI tools.
rows.new_rationale: The work is almost entirely performed within digital publishing software, focusing on the structured integration of text and graphics according to established design parameters. While some collaboration with writers and designers is required, the core tasks are highly procedural and increasingly susceptible to automation through AI-driven layout and formatting tools.

rows.title: Medical dosimetrists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves complex mathematical calculations, image analysis (CT/MRI), and digital treatment planning, all of which are domains where AI and machine learning excel. While there is a minor physical component involving patient positioning and equipment calibration, the vast majority of the work is digital information processing that is highly susceptible to automation and AI-driven optimization.
rows.new_rationale: The core of the role involves highly digital, data-intensive tasks like processing medical scans and performing complex radiation calculations. While the work is heavily reliant on software, it carries extreme professional accountability for patient safety, requiring high-stakes human judgment that is difficult to fully automate.

rows.title: Genetic counselors
rows.old_rationale: Genetic counselors perform high-level knowledge work, including analyzing complex DNA data and medical histories, which is highly susceptible to AI optimization. However, the core of the role involves providing psychological support and navigating sensitive ethical decisions with patients, requiring a level of empathy and human connection that limits full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While genetic counselors rely on digital records and genetic data analysis, the core of the role is deeply rooted in high-stakes, empathetic human interaction and clinical decision-making. The necessity for professional judgment, emotional support, and ethical accountability in interpreting life-altering medical information makes the role highly resistant to full automation.

rows.title: Historians
rows.old_rationale: Historians perform high-level knowledge work—researching, analyzing, and writing—that is increasingly digital and highly susceptible to AI-driven efficiency gains in document processing and synthesis. While the job requires human judgment and physical interaction with artifacts or archives, the core output of reports and articles faces significant restructuring as AI tools become capable of parsing vast historical datasets and drafting complex narratives.
rows.new_rationale: Historians perform significant digital research and writing, but their work is deeply rooted in the interpretation of physical artifacts and primary source documents that require expert, high-stakes judgment. While information processing is a core task, the synthesis of historical narrative and the preservation of physical sites involve nuanced human expertise that is difficult to fully automate.

rows.title: Sociologists
rows.old_rationale: Sociology is a knowledge-intensive field where core tasks like data analysis, literature reviews, and report writing are highly susceptible to AI enhancement. While qualitative field research and interpersonal interviews provide a buffer, the shift toward digital data collection and AI-driven statistical modeling significantly increases worker productivity and reshapes the research process.
rows.new_rationale: Sociologists rely heavily on digital tools for statistical analysis and report generation, and much of their research involves structured information processing. However, the role requires significant human interaction for interviews and collaboration, as well as high-level professional judgment to synthesize complex social data into actionable policy recommendations.

rows.title: Agricultural engineers
rows.old_rationale: Agricultural engineering is a hybrid role that combines digital design and data analysis with physical site visits and hardware testing. While AI will significantly automate core tasks like CAD modeling, environmental simulation, and precision farming data analysis, the requirement for physical oversight of construction, equipment testing in the field, and real-world troubleshooting provides a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: Agricultural engineers rely heavily on digital design and simulation software, but their work is fundamentally anchored in physical-world applications like machinery, livestock facilities, and environmental management. The role requires high-stakes professional judgment and accountability, particularly for licensed engineers, while balancing technical analysis with the need to coordinate across diverse stakeholder groups.

rows.title: Geographers
rows.old_rationale: Geographers primarily perform digital knowledge work, including data analysis, GIS modeling, and report writing, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. While some fieldwork exists, the core of the profession involves processing satellite imagery and census data—tasks where AI and machine learning are rapidly becoming more efficient than humans.
rows.new_rationale: Geographers rely heavily on digital tools like GIS, remote sensing, and data modeling to process large datasets, making their work product highly digital. While they perform fieldwork, the core analytical and reporting tasks are increasingly automated, though they still require professional judgment to interpret complex spatial relationships and provide high-stakes advice to government and business stakeholders.

rows.title: Military careers
rows.old_rationale: Military careers are a vast composite of physical combat, manual labor, and high-level digital strategy. While AI will heavily automate back-office administration, logistics, and intelligence analysis (high exposure), the core requirements of physical presence, tactical human leadership, and operation in unpredictable physical environments provide a significant buffer.
rows.new_rationale: Military careers are fundamentally defined by high-stakes, real-world physical operations and the necessity of human leadership, trust, and team coordination. While many roles involve digital systems and information processing, the core mission-critical tasks are deeply tied to physical presence, ethical accountability, and complex, non-routine decision-making that cannot be delegated to AI.

rows.title: Food and beverage serving and related workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, such as clearing tables, carrying heavy trays, and cleaning, which are difficult to automate with current robotics. However, AI is increasingly handling digital peripheral tasks like order-taking via kiosks or voice-AI drive-thrus and managing reservations, which reduces the need for human staff in front-of-house roles.
