rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly dependent on physical presence, manual labor, and the real-time handling of food and dining environments. While digital point-of-sale systems are used, the primary value is derived from interpersonal service and physical tasks that are difficult to automate without significant robotics integration.

rows.title: Home health and personal care aides
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are fundamentally physical and interpersonal, involving manual tasks like bathing, dressing, and lifting patients in unpredictable home environments. While AI may assist with peripheral administrative tasks like scheduling or monitoring vital signs, it cannot replace the essential human presence and physical labor required for personal care.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is physical, hands-on care and emotional support, which requires constant physical presence and high-touch human interaction. While there is some documentation and reporting, the work is fundamentally defined by the physical needs of the client and the necessity of human empathy and trust, making it highly resistant to AI automation.

rows.title: Secretaries and administrative assistants
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation—scheduling, drafting correspondence, managing databases, and transcribing—are fundamentally digital and align perfectly with the capabilities of LLMs and AI agents. While some roles require physical presence for visitor greeting or office management, the vast majority of tasks are routine information processing that can be automated or significantly streamlined, leading to major restructuring and reduced headcount per organization.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role involves high-volume digital information processing, such as document preparation, scheduling, and database management, which are highly susceptible to automation. While the role requires interpersonal skills and some situational judgment, the primary tasks are increasingly being handled by software and AI tools, leading to a projected stagnation in employment growth.

rows.title: Cooks
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job involves physical dexterity, sensory judgment (taste and smell), and real-time adaptation in a high-heat, unpredictable kitchen environment. While AI-driven automation is already impacting fast food and routine institutional cooking, high-end restaurant and private household cooking remain protected by the need for complex manual skills and human creativity.
rows.new_rationale: The core work of a cook is highly physical, involving manual manipulation of ingredients and equipment in a real-world environment. While some routine processes exist, the job relies heavily on sensory input and physical dexterity that is difficult to replicate digitally, and the role has very low digital or information-processing requirements.

rows.title: Waiters and waitresses
rows.old_rationale: While AI and automation are increasingly handling digital tasks like order-taking and payments via kiosks or mobile apps, the core of the job remains physical and interpersonal. The physical requirements of carrying trays, clearing tables, and providing real-time human hospitality in a dynamic environment create a significant barrier to full AI replacement.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly dependent on physical presence, manual labor, and direct interpersonal interaction with customers. While digital point-of-sale systems are used, the primary value proposition is the physical delivery of service and the human element of hospitality, which remains difficult to automate fully.

rows.title: Information clerks
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is highly exposed because its core tasks—processing routine data, answering inquiries, and maintaining records—are fundamentally digital and information-based. While some roles like hotel desk clerks or airport agents require a physical presence, the majority of the work involves routine information processing that is increasingly being automated by AI-driven self-service systems, chatbots, and automated data entry tools.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation involves highly routine, rule-based information processing and data entry, which are primary targets for automation. While some roles require physical presence or interpersonal interaction, the fundamental tasks of record-keeping, scheduling, and processing standard inquiries are increasingly handled by digital self-service systems.

rows.title: Material recording clerks
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is highly exposed because its core functions—tracking data, scheduling, and inventory reporting—are digital information-processing tasks that AI and automated systems excel at. While some physical presence is required to inspect goods or handle packages, the rapid integration of AI-driven logistics software, RFID technology, and computer vision for quality control is already leading to a projected decline in employment.
rows.new_rationale: The role is highly exposed to automation because it centers on routine information processing and data entry, which are increasingly handled by RFID and automated tracking systems. While the job requires physical presence in warehouses to handle goods and inspect items, the core administrative tasks are highly digitized and structured, making them prime targets for AI-driven optimization.

rows.title: Carpenters
rows.old_rationale: Carpentry is a highly physical occupation requiring manual dexterity, strength, and real-time problem-solving in unpredictable physical environments. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like blueprint analysis, material estimation, or business management, the core work of cutting, fitting, and installing materials remains resistant to automation.
rows.new_rationale: Carpentry is a highly physical, manual trade that requires significant on-site presence and dexterity, making it largely resistant to AI automation. While digital tools are used for planning and measurement, the core work involves physical manipulation of materials and high-stakes structural integrity, which requires human judgment and accountability.

rows.title: Financial managers
rows.old_rationale: Financial management is a predominantly digital knowledge-work occupation focused on data analysis, reporting, and forecasting, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. While the role requires significant human judgment, strategic advisory, and leadership, the core technical tasks of monitoring financial details and preparing statements are being rapidly transformed by AI tools.
rows.new_rationale: Financial managers operate almost entirely within digital environments, processing large volumes of structured financial data and reports. While much of the analytical and reporting work is routine, the role requires high-stakes judgment, strategic decision-making, and significant interpersonal coordination with top executives, which are difficult to fully automate.

rows.title: Agricultural workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, animal handling, and operating machinery in unpredictable outdoor environments, which provides a strong natural barrier to AI. While AI and robotics are increasingly used for precision agriculture and automated harvesting, the high cost of hardware and the need for manual dexterity in varied conditions mean human workers remain essential for the majority of tasks.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is manual labor, physical interaction with crops and livestock, and the operation of machinery in outdoor environments. While some record-keeping exists, the work is overwhelmingly defined by physical presence and manual dexterity rather than digital or information-based tasks.

rows.title: Bartenders
rows.old_rationale: The core of bartending involves physical manipulation of objects, manual cleaning, and real-time social interaction in a physical environment. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like inventory management or automated ordering, the primary value of the role lies in human presence, physical service, and the social 'hospitality' aspect which remains difficult to automate.
rows.new_rationale: Bartending is highly dependent on physical presence, manual dexterity, and real-world interaction, making it resistant to automation in its core service delivery. While some information processing occurs, the role relies heavily on interpersonal engagement and high-stakes, real-time judgment regarding customer safety and legal compliance.

rows.title: Barbers, hairstylists, and cosmetologists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves manual dexterity and physical interaction in a highly unpredictable environment (human hair and skin). While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like appointment scheduling, marketing, or visualizing hairstyles for clients, the physical act of cutting, coloring, and styling hair remains a significant barrier to automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, requiring direct, manual interaction with clients in a real-world setting. While there is some administrative work, the primary value is delivered through interpersonal service and skilled physical labor that cannot be digitized or automated.

rows.title: Quality control inspectors
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical inspection and data analysis. While AI-powered computer vision systems are rapidly automating the visual detection of defects, the role still requires significant physical presence to handle materials, operate manual gauges, and perform mechanical tests that robots cannot yet fully replicate in diverse manufacturing environments.
rows.new_rationale: The role is heavily centered on physical interaction with products, materials, and machinery in a manufacturing environment, necessitating a high physical world dependency. While the job involves routine data recording and the use of digital inspection tools, the core task of identifying physical defects requires manual manipulation and sensory observation that remains difficult to fully automate.

rows.title: Construction equipment operators
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical operation of heavy machinery in unpredictable, outdoor environments that require real-time human coordination and manual maintenance. While AI and automation are increasingly integrated into equipment via GPS and computerized controls, the physical nature of the work and the need for on-site safety judgment provide a significant barrier to full automation in the near term.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is the physical manipulation of heavy machinery in real-world environments, which is highly resistant to automation despite the increasing use of GPS and computerized controls. The work involves minimal information processing or digital output, and while it requires coordination with a crew, it does not rely on complex interpersonal relationships or high-stakes professional judgment in the way that knowledge-based roles do.

rows.title: Industrial machinery mechanics, machinery maintenance workers, and millwrights
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and real-time troubleshooting in unpredictable physical environments, which provides a strong barrier against AI automation. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic capabilities and predictive maintenance scheduling, the actual repair, installation, and physical manipulation of heavy machinery still require human presence and mechanical skill.
rows.new_rationale: This occupation is fundamentally rooted in the physical world, requiring manual manipulation of heavy machinery, welding, and site-specific repairs that cannot be digitized. While they use diagnostic software and technical manuals, the core value of the role is the physical intervention and high-stakes judgment required to ensure industrial safety and operational continuity.

rows.title: Plumbers, pipefitters, and steamfitters
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are highly physical, requiring manual dexterity, strength, and real-time problem-solving in unpredictable physical environments like crawl spaces and construction sites. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like cost estimation, blueprint analysis via BIM, and scheduling, it cannot perform the essential hands-on installation and repair work.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this occupation is manual, site-specific labor involving the physical installation and repair of complex systems in unpredictable environments. While digital tools like BIM are used for planning, the actual execution requires high-stakes physical judgment, safety compliance, and manual dexterity that are currently beyond the scope of AI automation.

rows.title: Administrative services and facilities managers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical site management. While AI can significantly automate administrative tasks like recordkeeping, supply procurement, and energy pattern analysis, the role requires a physical presence to inspect facilities, oversee manual labor (janitors, repair workers), and manage real-world safety and security compliance.
rows.new_rationale: This role balances digital administrative tasks and data management with significant physical oversight of facilities and infrastructure. Success relies heavily on interpersonal leadership, coordination of diverse staff, and high-stakes judgment regarding safety, compliance, and organizational efficiency.

rows.title: Dental assistants
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job involves physical, hands-on tasks such as sterilizing instruments, assisting in surgeries, and providing direct patient care that requires human presence and manual dexterity. While AI can significantly automate the administrative and digital aspects of the role—such as scheduling, billing, and preliminary X-ray analysis—the majority of the workday is spent in the physical dental operatory where AI cannot currently replace human labor.
rows.new_rationale: The core of this role is highly physical, requiring manual dexterity, direct patient interaction, and the physical handling of instruments in a clinical setting. While there is some digital recordkeeping and scheduling, the primary value is derived from real-time, in-person assistance and patient care that cannot be automated.

rows.title: Clinical laboratory technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical laboratory work and digital data analysis. While AI and computer vision are rapidly automating the interpretation of slides and test results (high exposure), the physical requirements of preparing specimens, maintaining complex hardware, and interacting with patients provide a significant barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the role involves significant digital data entry and automated analysis, the core of the work is physically manipulating biological specimens and maintaining laboratory equipment in a real-world clinical setting. The high accountability for diagnostic accuracy and the need for manual dexterity and physical presence in a lab environment make this role resistant to full automation, despite the increasing use of automated testing equipment.

rows.old_rationale: Nuclear technicians perform a mix of digital monitoring and physical labor, such as collecting environmental samples and maintaining hardware. While AI can significantly automate the data analysis, radiation calculation, and system monitoring aspects of the role, the requirement for physical presence in hazardous environments and the manual calibration of equipment provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While nuclear technicians perform significant data monitoring and calculation, the core of their work is highly dependent on physical presence, manual equipment maintenance, and onsite safety operations in hazardous environments. The role carries high-stakes accountability for safety and regulatory compliance, which limits the extent to which AI can replace the physical and judgmental aspects of the job.
